text.DRV: 4:14 And he commanded me at that time that I should teach you the ceremonies and judgments, which you should do in the Land, that you shall possess.

text.NRSV: Since you saw no form when the Lord spoke to you at Horeb out of the fire, take care and watch yourselves closely,
text.Rotherham: Ye must take diligent heed, therefore unto your own souls, for ye saw no manner of form, on the day Yahweh spake unto you in Horeb out of the midst of the fire;
text.KJV: 4:15 Take ye therfore good heed vnto your selues, (for ye saw no maner of similitude on the day that the Lord spake vnto you in Horeb, out of the midst of the fire)
text.DRV: 4:15 Keep therefore your souls carefully. You saw not any similitude in the day, that our Lord spake to you in Horeb from the midst of the fire:

text.NRSV: so that you do not act corruptly by making an idol for yourselves, in the form of any figure—the likeness of male or female,
text.Rotherham: lest ye should break faith, and make you an image, a form of any likeness,—a model of male or female;
text.KJV: 4:16 Lest yee corrupt your selues, and make you a grauen image, the similitude of any figure, the likenes of male, or female,
text.DRV: 4:16 lest perhaps deceived you might make you a graven similitude, or image of male or female,

text.NRSV: the likeness of any animal that is on the earth, the likeness of any winged bird that flies in the air,
text.Rotherham: a model of any beast that is in the earth,—a model of any winged bird, that flieth in the heavens;
text.KJV: 4:17 The likenesse of any beast that is on the earth, the likenes of any winged foule that flieth in the aire,
text.DRV: 4:17 the similitude of all cattle, that are upon the earth, or of birds, that fly under heaven,

text.NRSV: the likeness of anything that creeps on the ground, the likeness of any fish that is in the water under the earth.
text.Rotherham: a model of any thing that creepeth on the ground,—a model of any fish that is in the waters beneath the earth;
text.KJV: 4:18 The likenesse of any thing that creepeth on the ground, the likenesse of any fish that is in the waters beneath the earth:
text.DRV: 4:18 and of creeping beasts, that move on the earth, or of fishes, that under the earth abide in the waters:

text.NRSV: And when you look up to the heavens and see the sun, the moon, and the stars, all the host of heaven, do not be led astray and bow down to them and serve them, things that the Lord your God has allotted to all the peoples everywhere under heaven.
text.Rotherham: or lest thou shouldest lift up thine eyes towards the heavens and see the sun and the moon, and the stars—all the host of the heavens, and shouldest be seduced, and shouldest bow thyself down to them and he led to serve them,—the which Yahweh thy God hath assigned unto all the peoples under all the heavens;
text.KJV: 4:19 And lest thou lift vp thine eyes vnto heauen, and when thou seest the sun, and the moone, and the starres, euen all the hoste of heauen, shouldest be driuen to worship them, and serue them, which the Lord thy God hath diuided vnto all nations vnder the whole heauen.
text.DRV: 4:19 lest perhaps lifting up thine eyes to heaven, thou see the Sun and the Moon, and all the stars of heaven, and deceived by error thou adore and serve them, which the Lord thy God created to serve all nations, that are under heaven.

text.NRSV: But the Lord has taken you and brought you out of the iron-smelter, out of Egypt, to become a people of his very own possession, as you are now.
text.Rotherham: whereas you, hath Yahweh taken, and brought you forth out of a smelting-pot of iron, out of Egypt,—that ye might become his own inherited people as at this day.
text.KJV: 4:20 But the Lord hath taken you, and brought you foorth out of the yron fornace, euen out of Egypt, to bee vnto him a people of inheritance, as ye are this day.
text.DRV: 4:20 But you our Lord hath taken, and brought out of the iron furnace of Ægypt, to have you his people by inheritance, as it is this present day.

text.NRSV: The Lord was angry with me because of you, and he vowed that I should not cross the Jordan and that I should not enter the good land that the Lord your God is giving for your possession.
text.Rotherham: But, Yahweh shewed himself angry with me, for your sakes,—and sware that I should not pass over the Jordan, and that I should not enter into the good land, which, Yahweh thy God, is giving unto thee as an inheritance;
text.KJV: 4:21 Furthermore, the Lord was angry with mee for your sakes, and sware that I should not goe ouer Iordan, and that I should not goe in vnto that good land which the Lord thy God giueth thee for an inheritance.
text.DRV: 4:21 And our Lord was angry with me for your words, and he sware that I should not pass over Jordan, nor enter into the excellent Land, which he will give you.

text.NRSV: For I am going to die in this land without crossing over the Jordan, but you are going to cross over to take possession of that good land.
text.Rotherham: for, I, am about to die in this land, I am not to pass over the Jordan,—but, ye, are to pass over, and possess this good land.
text.KJV: 4:22 But I must die in this lande, I must not goe ouer Iordan: but ye shall goe ouer and possesse that good land.
text.DRV: 4:22 Behold I die in this ground, I shall not pass over Jordan: you shall pass, and possess the goodly Land.

text.NRSV: So be careful not to forget the covenant that the Lord your God made with you, and not to make for yourselves an idol in the form of anything that the Lord your God has forbidden you.
text.Rotherham: Take heed to yourselves lest ye forget the covenant of Yahweh your God, which he hath solemnised with you,—and so make for yourselves an image, the form of anything, the which Yahweh thy God hath forbidden thee;
text.KJV: 4:23 Take heed vnto your selues, lest ye forget the couenant of the Lord your God, which hee made with you, and make you a grauen image, or the likenes of any thing which the Lord thy God hath forbidden thee.
text.DRV: 4:23 Beware lest at any time thou forget the covenant of the Lord thy God, which he hath made with thee: and make to thee a graven similitude of those things, which our Lord hath prohibited to be made:

text.NRSV: For the Lord your God is a devouring fire, a jealous God.
text.Rotherham: seeing that as for Yahweh thy God, a consuming fire, he is,—a jealous GOD.
text.KJV: 4:24 For the Lord thy God is a consuming fire, euen a iealous God.
text.DRV: 4:24 because the Lord thy God is a consuming fire, jealous God.

text.NRSV: When you have had children and children’s children, and become complacent in the land, if you act corruptly by making an idol in the form of anything, thus doing what is evil in the sight of the Lord your God, and provoking him to anger,
text.Rotherham: When thou shalt beget sons, and sons’ sons, and become careless in the land and break faith and make an image the form of any thing, and shalt do the thing that is wicked in the eyes of Yahweh thy God provoking him to anger,
text.KJV: 4:25 ¶ When thou shalt beget children, and childrens children, and shalt haue remained long in the land, and shal corrupt your selues, & make a grauen image, or the likenes of any thing, and shall doe euil in the sight of the Lord thy God, to prouoke him to anger:
text.DRV: 4:25 If you shall beget sons & nephews and abide in the Land, & being deceived make to you some similitude, committing evil before the Lord your God, to provoke him to wrath:

text.NRSV: I call heaven and earth to witness against you today that you will soon utterly perish from the land that you are crossing the Jordan to occupy; you will not live long on it, but will be utterly destroyed.
text.Rotherham: I take both the heavens and the earth to witness against you to-day, that ye shall, perish, speedily, from off the land which ye are passing over the Jordan to possess,—ye shall not prolong your days thereupon; for, ye shall surely be laid waste;
text.KJV: 4:26 I call heauen and earth to witnesse against you this day, that ye shall soone vtterly perish from off the land whereunto you goe ouer Iordan, to possesse it: yee shall not prolong your dayes vpon it, but shall vtterly bee destroyed.
text.DRV: 4:26 I call this day heaven and earth witnesses, that you shall quickly perish from out of the Land, which being passed over Jordan you shall possess. You shall not dwell therein long time, but our Lord will destroy you,

text.NRSV: The Lord will scatter you among the peoples; only a few of you will be left among the nations where the Lord will lead you.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh will scatter you among the peoples,—and ye shall have left remaining of you men easily counted, among the nations whither Yahweh will drive you;
text.KJV: 4:27 And the Lord shall scatter you among the nations, and ye shall be left few in number among the heathen, whither the Lord shall lead you.
text.DRV: 4:27 and disperse you into all nations, and you shall remain a few among the nations, to the which our Lord will lead you.

text.NRSV: There you will serve other gods made by human hands, objects of wood and stone that neither see, nor hear, nor eat, nor smell.
text.Rotherham: and ye will serve there gods made by the hands of man,—wood or stone, which neither see nor hear nor eat nor smell.
text.KJV: 4:28 And there ye shall serue gods, the worke of mens hands, wood and stone, which neither see, nor heare, nor eate, nor smell.
text.DRV: 4:28 and there you shall serve gods, that were framed with men's hand, wood and stone, that see not, nor hear, nor eat, nor smell.

text.NRSV: From there you will seek the Lord your God, and you will find him if you search after him with all your heart and soul.
text.Rotherham: If thou shalt seek from thence, Yahweh thy God, then shalt thou find him,—when thou shalt search after him with all thy heart, and with all thy soul.
text.KJV: 4:29 But if from thence thou shalt seeke the Lord thy God, thou shalt finde him, if thou seeke him with all thy heart, and with all thy soule.
text.DRV: 4:29 And when thou shalt seek there the Lord thy God, thou shalt find him, yet so, if thou seek him with all thy heart, & all tribulation of thy soul.

text.NRSV: In your distress, when all these things have happened to you in time to come, you will return to the Lord your God and heed him.
text.Rotherham: In thy distress, when all these things have found thee out—in the afterpart of the days, thou wilt return unto Yahweh thy God, and wilt hearken to his voice.
text.KJV: 4:30 When thou art in tribulation, and all these things are come vpon thee, euen in the latter dayes, if thou turne to the Lord thy God, and shalt be obedient vnto his voice:
text.DRV: 4:30 After that all the things aforesaid shall find thee, & in the latter time thou shalt return to the Lord thy God, and shalt hear his voice.

text.NRSV: Because the Lord your God is a merciful God, he will neither abandon you nor destroy you; he will not forget the covenant with your ancestors that he swore to them.
text.Rotherham: For, a GOD of compassion, is Yahweh thy God, he will not forsake thee neither will he destroy thee,—nor forget the covenant of thy fathers which he sware unto them.
text.KJV: 4:31 (For the Lord thy God is a mercifull God) he will not forsake thee, neither destroy thee, nor forget the couenant of thy fathers, which he sware vnto them.
text.DRV: 4:31 Because the Lord thy God is a merciful God: he will not leave thee, nor altogether destroy thee; nor forget the covenant, wherein he sware to thy fathers.

text.NRSV: For ask now about former ages, long before your own, ever since the day that God created human beings on the earth; ask from one end of heaven to the other: has anything so great as this ever happened or has its like ever been heard of?
text.Rotherham: For ask, I pray you, of the former days which were before thee, even from the day when God created man upon the earth, yea from one end of the heavens even to the other end of the heavens,—whether aught was ever brought to pass, like this great thing, or was ever heard of like it:
text.KJV: 4:32 For aske now of the dayes that are past, which were before thee, since the day that God created man vpon earth, and aske from the one side of heauen vnto the other, whether there hath bene any such thing as this great thing is, or hath bene heard like it?
text.DRV: 4:32 Ask of the days of old, that have been before thy time from the day that God created man upon the earth, from one end of heaven to the other end thereof, if ever there was done the like thing, or it hath been known at any time,

text.NRSV: Has any people ever heard the voice of a god speaking out of a fire, as you have heard, and lived?
text.Rotherham: Did a people ever hear the voice of a god speaking out of the midst of fire, as, thou, didst hear, and yet live?
text.KJV: 4:33 Did euer people heare the voyce of God speaking out of the midst of the fire, as thou hast heard, and liue?
text.DRV: 4:33 that a people should hear the voice of God speaking out of the midst of fire, as thou hast heard, and lived:

text.NRSV: Or has any god ever attempted to go and take a nation for himself from the midst of another nation, by trials, by signs and wonders, by war, by a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, and by terrifying displays of power, as the Lord your God did for you in Egypt before your very eyes?
text.Rotherham: Or did a god ever make trial of entering in to take unto him a nation out of the midst of a nation, by provings, by signs and by wonders and by fighting, and by a firm hand and by a stretched-out arm, and by great terrors,—according to all that Yahweh your God did for you in Egypt, before your eyes?
text.KJV: 4:34 Or hath God assayed to goe and take him a nation from the midst of another nation, by temptations, by signes, and by wonders, and by warre, and by a mighty hand, and by a stretehed out arme, and by great terrors, according to all that the Lord your God did for you in Egypt before your eyes?
text.DRV: 4:34 if God so did that he went in, and took unto him a Nation out of the midst of nations, by temptations, signs, and wonders, by fight and strong hand, and stretched out arm, and horrible visions according to all things, that the Lord your God did for you in Ægypt, thine eyes seeing it:

text.NRSV: To you it was shown so that you would acknowledge that the Lord is God; there is no other besides him.
text.Rotherham: Thou, wast allowed to see that thou mightest know, that Yahweh, he is God, there is none other than he alone.
text.KJV: 4:35 Unto thee it was shewed, that thou mightest know, that the Lord hee is God; there is none else besides him.
text.DRV: 4:35 that thou mightest know that our Lord, he is God, and there is none other beside him,

text.NRSV: From heaven he made you hear his voice to discipline you. On earth he showed you his great fire, while you heard his words coming out of the fire.
text.Rotherham: Out of the heavens, he let thee hear his voice to instruct thee,—and, upon the earth, he let thee see his great fire, and his own words, thou didst hear out of the midst of the fire.
text.KJV: 4:36 Out of heauen hee made thee to heare his voice, that he might instruct thee: and vpon earth hee shewed thee his great fire, and thou heardest his words out of the midst of the fire.
text.DRV: 4:36 from heaven he made thee to hear his voice, that he might teach thee. And in earth he shewed thee his fire, very great, and thou didst hear his words out of the midst of the fire,

text.NRSV: And because he loved your ancestors, he chose their descendants after them. He brought you out of Egypt with his own presence, by his great power,
text.Rotherham: And because that he loved thy fathers, therefore chose he his seed after him,—and brought thee forth with his presence with his great might, out of Egypt;
text.KJV: 4:37 And because he loued thy fathers, therefore he chose their seed after them, and brought thee out in his sight with his mightie power out of Egypt:
text.DRV: 4:37 because he loved thy fathers, and chose their seed after them. And he brought thee out of Ægypt, going before thee in his great power,

text.NRSV: driving out before you nations greater and mightier than yourselves, to bring you in, giving you their land for a possession, as it is still today.
text.Rotherham: to dispossess nations, greater and stronger than thou from before thee,—to bring thee in. to give unto thee their land for an inheritance: As at this day.
text.KJV: 4:38 To driue out nations from before thee, greater and mightier then thou art, to bring thee in, to giue thee their land for an inheritance, as it is this day.
text.DRV: 4:38 to destroy very great nations and stronger than thou at thy entering in, and to bring thee in, and give thee their land in possession, as thou seest this present day.

text.NRSV: So acknowledge today and take to heart that the Lord is God in heaven above and on the earth beneath; there is no other.
text.Rotherham: So then thou must consider to-day, and bring it back to thy heart, that, Yahweh, he is God in the heavens above and upon the earth beneath,—there is none other.
text.KJV: 4:39 Know therefore this day, & consider it in thine heart, that the Lord hee is God in heauen aboue, and vpon the earth beneath: there is none else.
text.DRV: 4:39 know therefore this day, and think in thy heart that our Lord he is God in heaven above, and in the earth beneath, and there is none other.

text.NRSV: Keep his statutes and his commandments, which I am commanding you today for your own well-being and that of your descendants after you, so that you may long remain in the land that the Lord your God is giving you for all time.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou keep his statutes and his commandments which, I, am commanding thee to-day, that it may go well with thee, and with thy sons after thee,—and to the end thou mayest prolong thy days upon the soil, which Yahweh thy God, is giving thee, all the days.
text.KJV: 4:40 Thou shalt keepe therefore his Statutes, and his Commandements, which I command thee this day; that it may goe well with thee, and with thy children after thee, and that thou mayest prolong thy dayes vpon the earth, which the Lord thy God giueth thee, for euer.
text.DRV: 4:40 Keep his precepts and commandments, which I command thee: that it may be well with thee, and thy children after thee, and thou mayest remain a long time upon the Land, which the Lord thy God will give thee.

text.NRSV: Then Moses set apart on the east side of the Jordan three cities
text.Rotherham: Then, did Moses set apart three cities, over the Jordan,—towards the rising of the sun:
text.KJV: 4:41 ¶ Then Moses seuered three cities on this side Iordan, toward the Sunne rising:
text.DRV: 4:41 Then Moyses separated three cities beyond Jordan at the east side,

text.NRSV: to which a homicide could flee, someone who unintentionally kills another person, the two not having been at enmity before; the homicide could flee to one of these cities and live:
text.Rotherham: that the manslayer might flee thither who should slay his neighbour unawares, he, not having hated him, aforetime,—and might flee unto one of these cities, and live:
text.KJV: 4:42 That the slayer might flee thither, which should kill his neighbour vnawares, and hated him not in times past, and that fleeing vnto one of these cities he might liue:
text.DRV: 4:42 that he might flee to them which should kill his neighbour not voluntarily, neither was his enemy a day or two before, and he might scape to some of these cities:

text.NRSV: Bezer in the wilderness on the tableland belonging to the Reubenites, Ramoth in Gilead belonging to the Gadites, and Golan in Bashan belonging to the Manassites.
text.Rotherham: namely Bezer, in the desert of the table-land, for the Reubenites,—and Ramoth in Gilead for the Gadites, and Golan in Bashan, for the Manassites.
text.KJV: 4:43 Namely, Bezer in the wildernesse, in the plaine countrey of the Reubenites; and Ramoth in Gilead of the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan, of the Manassites.
text.DRV: 4:43 Bosor in the wilderness, which is situated in the champion country of the tribe of Ruben: and Ramoth in Galaad, which is in the tribe of Gad: and Golan in Basan, which is in the tribe of Manasses.

text.NRSV: This is the law that Moses set before the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: This, then is the law which Moses set before the sons of Israel:
text.KJV: 4:44 ¶ And this is the Law which Moses set before the children of Israel:
text.DRV: 4:44 This is the law that Moyses set before the children of Israel,

text.NRSV: These are the decrees and the statutes and ordinances that Moses spoke to the Israelites when they had come out of Egypt,
text.Rotherham: These, are the testimonies, and the statutes and the regulations,—which Moses spake unto the sons of Israel when they came forth out of Egypt:
text.KJV: 4:45 These are the Testimonies, and the Statutes, and the Iudgements, which Moses spake vnto the children of Israel, after they came foorth out of Egypt;
text.DRV: 4:45 and these are the testimonies and ceremonies and judgments, which he spake to the children of Israel, when they came out of Ægypt,

text.NRSV: beyond the Jordan in the valley opposite Beth-peor, in the land of King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned at Heshbon, whom Moses and the Israelites defeated when they came out of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: over the Jordan in the valley over against Beth-peor, in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon,—whom Moses and the sons of Israel smote, when they came forth out of Egypt:
text.KJV: 4:46 On this side Iordan in the valley ouer against Beth-Peor, in the land of Sihon King of the Amorites, who dwelt at Heshbon, whom Moses and the children of Israel smote, after they were come foorth out of Egypt.
text.DRV: 4:46 beyond Jordan in the valley against the temple of Phogor in the land of Sehon king of the Ammorrheite, that dwelt in Hesebon, whom Moyses struck. The children of Israel also coming out of Ægypt

text.NRSV: They occupied his land and the land of King Og of Bashan, the two kings of the Amorites on the eastern side of the Jordan:
text.Rotherham: so they took possession of his land, and the land of Og king of Bashan the two kings of the Amorites, who were over the Jordan,—towards the rising of the sun;
text.KJV: 4:47 And they possessed his land, and the land of Og king of Bashan, two kings of the Amorites, which were on this side Iordan toward the sun rising,
text.DRV: 4:47 possessed his land, and the land of Og the king of Basan, the two kings of the Amorrheites, which were beyond Jordan toward the rising of the sun:

text.NRSV: from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, as far as Mount Sirion (that is, Hermon),
text.Rotherham: from Aroer which is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon, even unto Mount Sion, the same, is Hermon;
text.KJV: 4:48 From Aroer, which is by the banke of the riuer Arnon, euen vnto mount Sion, which is Hermon,
text.DRV: 4:48 from Aroer, which is situated upon the bank of the torrent Arnon, unto the mountain Sion, which is also Hermon,

text.NRSV: together with all the Arabah on the east side of the Jordan as far as the Sea of the Arabah, under the slopes of Pisgah.
text.Rotherham: and all the waste plain over the Jordan, towards sunrise, even unto the sea of the waste plain,—under the slopes of the Pisgah.
text.KJV: 4:49 And all the plaine of this side Iordan Eastward, euen vnto the sea, of the plaine vnder the springs of Pisgah.
text.DRV: 4:49 all the plain beyond Jordan at the east side, unto the sea of the wilderness, and unto the foot of mount Phasga.

text.NRSV: Moses convened all Israel, and said to them: Hear, O Israel, the statutes and ordinances that I am addressing to you today; you shall learn them and observe them diligently.
text.Rotherham: Then called Moses unto all Israel and said unto them—Hear, O Israel, the statutes and the regulations, which I am speaking in your ears to-day,—so shall ye learn them, and observe to do them.
text.KJV: 5:1 And Moses called all Israel, and sayd vnto them, Heare, O Israel, the Statutes and Iudgements which I speake in your eares this day, that ye may learne them, and keepe and doe them.
text.DRV: 5:1 And Moyses called all Israel, and said to them: Hear Israel the ceremonies and judgments, which I speak in your ears this day: learn them, and fulfill them in work.

text.NRSV: The Lord our God made a covenant with us at Horeb.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh our God, solemnised with us a covenant in Horeb:
text.KJV: 5:2 The Lord our God made a couenant with vs in Horeb.
text.DRV: 5:2 The Lord our God made a covenant with us in Horeb.

text.NRSV: Not with our ancestors did the Lord make this covenant, but with us, who are all of us here alive today.
text.Rotherham: not with our fathers did Yahweh solemnise this covenant,—but with us, ourselves—these here to-day, all of us living.
text.KJV: 5:3 The Lord made not this couenant with our fathers, but with vs: euen vs, who are all of vs here aliue this day.
text.DRV: 5:3 Not with our fathers did he make the covenant, but with us at this present, and do live.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke with you face to face at the mountain, out of the fire.
text.Rotherham: Face to face, spake Yahweh with you in the mount out of the midst of the fire,
text.KJV: 5:4 The Lord talked with you, face to face, in the mount, out of the midst of the fire,
text.DRV: 5:4 Face to face did he speak to us in the mount out of the midst of the fire.

text.NRSV: (At that time I was standing between the Lord and you to declare to you the words of the Lord; for you were afraid because of the fire and did not go up the mountain.) And he said:
text.Rotherham: (I was standing between Yahweh and you, at that time, to declare unto you the word of Yahweh,—for ye shrank with fear from the presence of the fire, and went not up in the mount) saying:—
text.KJV: 5:5 (I stood betweene the Lord and you, at that time, to shew you the word of the Lord: for ye were afraid by reason of the fire, and went not vp into the mount,) saying,
text.DRV: 5:5 I was arbiter and Mediator betwixt our Lord and you at that time, to shew you his words, for you feared the fire, and went not up into the mount, and he said:

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery;
text.Rotherham: I, am Yahweh thy God, who have brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt out of the house of servants:—
text.KJV: 5:6 ¶ I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee out of the lande of Egypt, from the house of bondage.
text.DRV: 5:6 I the Lord thy God, that brought thee out of the Land of Ægypt out of the house of servitude.

text.NRSV: you shall have no other gods before me.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not have other gods, besides me:
text.KJV: 5:7 Thou shalt haue none other gods before me.
text.DRV: 5:7 Thou shalt not have strange gods in my sight.

text.NRSV: You shall not make for yourself an idol, whether in the form of anything that is in heaven above, or that is on the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not make unto thee an image, any form that is in the heavens above, or that is in the earth beneath or that is in the waters beneath the earth:
text.KJV: 5:8 Thou shalt not make thee any grauen image, or any likenesse of any thing that is in heauen aboue, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the waters beneath the earth.
text.DRV: 5:8 Thou shalt not make to thee a thing graven, nor the similitude of any things, that are in heaven above, and that are in the earth beneath, and that abide in the waters under the earth.

text.NRSV: You shall not bow down to them or worship them; for I the Lord your God am a jealous God, punishing children for the iniquity of parents, to the third and fourth generation of those who reject me,
text.Rotherham: thou shalt not bow thyself down to them nor be led to serve them,—for, I Yahweh thy God, am a jealous GOD, visiting the iniquity of fathers upon sons even unto three generations and unto four, unto them that hate me;
text.KJV: 5:9 Thou shalt not bow downe thy selfe vnto them, nor serue them: for I the Lord thy God am a ielous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers vpon the children, vnto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me,
text.DRV: 5:9 Thou shalt not adore them, and thou shalt not serve them. For I am the Lord thy God, a Jealous God, rendering the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation to them that hate me,

text.NRSV: but showing steadfast love to the thousandth generation of those who love me and keep my commandments.
text.Rotherham: but shewing lovingkindness unto a thousand generation—unto them who love me, and keep my commandments:
text.KJV: 5:10 And shewing mercy vnto thousands, of them that loue me, and keepe my commandements.
text.DRV: 5:10 and doing mercy upon many thousands to them that love me, and keep my precepts.

text.NRSV: You shall not make wrongful use of the name of the Lord your God, for the Lord will not acquit anyone who misuses his name.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not utter the name of Yahweh thy God, for falsehood,—for Yahweh will not let him go unpunished who uttereth his name for falsehood:
text.KJV: 5:11 Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vaine: for the Lord will not holde him guiltlesse that taketh his name in vaine.
text.DRV: 5:11 Thou shalt not usurp the name of the Lord thy God in vain: for he shall not be unpunished that taketh his name upon a vain thing.

text.NRSV: Observe the sabbath day and keep it holy, as the Lord your God commanded you.
text.Rotherham: Observe the sabbath day, to hallow it,—as Yahweh thy God, hath commanded thee:
text.KJV: 5:12 Keepe the Sabbath day to sanctifie it, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee.
text.DRV: 5:12 Observe the day of the Sabbath, to sanctify it, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee.

text.NRSV: Six days you shall labor and do all your work.
text.Rotherham: six days, shalt thou labour, and do all thy work;
text.KJV: 5:13 Sixe dayes thou shalt labour, and doe all thy worke.
text.DRV: 5:13 Six days shalt thou work, and shalt do all thy works.

text.NRSV: But the seventh day is a sabbath to the Lord your God; you shall not do any work—you, or your son or your daughter, or your male or female slave, or your ox or your donkey, or any of your livestock, or the resident alien in your towns, so that your male and female slave may rest as well as you.
text.Rotherham: but, the seventh day, is a sabbath unto Yahweh thy God,—thou shalt do no work—thou nor thy son nor thy daughter nor thy servant nor thy handmaid nor thine ox nor thine ass nor any of thy cattle, nor thy sojourner who is within thy gates, that thy servant and thy handmaid may rest, as well as thou.
text.KJV: 5:14 But the seuenth day is the Sabbath of the Lord thy God: in it thou shalt not doe any worke, thou, nor thy sonne, nor thy daughter, nor thy man seruant, nor thy maid seruant, nor thine oxe, nor thine asse, nor any of thy cattel, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates, that thy man seruant and thy maid seruant may rest as well as thou.
text.DRV: 5:14 The seventh is the day of the Sabbath, that is, the rest of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not do any work therein, thou, and thy son and daughter, man servant and woman servant, and oxe, and ass, and all thy cattle, and the stranger that is within thy gates: that thy man servant may rest, and thy woman servant, even as thyself.

text.NRSV: Remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt, and the Lord your God brought you out from there with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm; therefore the Lord your God commanded you to keep the sabbath day.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou remember that a servant, wast thou in the land of Egypt, and that Yahweh thy God brought thee forth from thence, with a firm hand, and with a stretched-out arm,—for this cause, hath Yahweh thy God commanded thee to keep the sabbath day:
text.KJV: 5:15 And remember that thou wast a seruant in the land of Egypt, and that the Lord thy God brought thee out thence, through a mightie hand, and by a stretched out arme: Therefore the Lord thy God commaunded thee to keepe the Sabbath day.
text.DRV: 5:15 Remember that thou also didst serve in Ægypt, and the Lord thy God brought thee out from thence in a strong hand, and stretched out arm. Therefore hath he commanded thee that thou shouldst observe the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: Honor your father and your mother, as the Lord your God commanded you, so that your days may be long and that it may go well with you in the land that the Lord your God is giving you.
text.Rotherham: Honour thy father and thy mother, as Yahweh thy God hath commanded thee,—that thy days may be prolonged and that it may go well with thee, upon the soil which Yahweh thy God is about to give unto thee:
text.KJV: 5:16 ¶ Honour thy father and thy mother, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee, that thy daies may be prolonged, and that it may goe well with thee, in the land which the Lord thy God giueth thee.
text.DRV: 5:16 Honour thy father and mother, as our Lord thy God hath commanded thee, that thou mayst live a long time, and it may be well with thee in the Land, which the Lord thy God will give thee.

text.NRSV: You shall not murder.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not commit murder:
text.KJV: 5:17 Thou shalt not kill.
text.DRV: 5:17 Thou shalt not murder.

text.NRSV: Neither shall you commit adultery.
text.Rotherham: Neither shalt thou commit adultery:
text.KJV: 5:18 Neither shalt thou commit adulterie.
text.DRV: 5:18 Neither shalt thou commit adultery.

text.NRSV: Neither shall you steal.
text.Rotherham: Neither shalt thou steal:
text.KJV: 5:19 Neither shalt thou steale.
text.DRV: 5:19 And thou shalt not steal.

text.NRSV: Neither shall you bear false witness against your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Neither shalt thou testify against thy neighbour with a witness of falsehood:
text.KJV: 5:20 Neither shalt thou beare false witnesse against thy neighbour.
text.DRV: 5:20 Neither shalt thou speak against thy neighbour false testimony.

text.NRSV: Neither shall you covet your neighbor’s wife. Neither shall you desire your neighbor’s house, or field, or male or female slave, or ox, or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Neither shalt thou covet thy neighbour’s wife,—neither shalt thou desire thy neighbour’s house, his field, or his servant, or his handmaid, his ox or his ass, or anything that is thy neighbour’s.
text.KJV: 5:21 Neither shalt thou desire thy neighbours wife, neither shalt thou couet thy neighbours house, his field, or his man seruant, or his maide seruant, his oxe, or his asse, or any thing that is thy neighbours.
text.DRV: 5:21 Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's wife: Nor house, nor field, nor man servant, nor woman servant, nor oxe, nor ass, and all things that are his.

text.NRSV: These words the Lord spoke with a loud voice to your whole assembly at the mountain, out of the fire, the cloud, and the thick darkness, and he added no more. He wrote them on two stone tablets, and gave them to me.
text.Rotherham: These words, spake Yahweh unto all the convocation of you in the mount out of the midst of the fire the cloud and the thick gloom, a loud voice, and added not,—and he wrote them upon two tables of stone, and gave them unto me.
text.KJV: 5:22 ¶ These wordes the Lord spake vnto all your assembly in the mount out of the midst of the fire, of the cloud, and of the thicke darkenesse, with a great voice, and he added no more, and he wrote them in two Tables of stone, and deliuered them vnto me.
text.DRV: 5:22 These words spake our Lord to all your multitude in the mount, out of the midst of the fire and the cloud, and the darkness, with a loud voice, adding nothing more: and he wrote them in the two tables of stone, which he delivered unto me.

text.NRSV: When you heard the voice out of the darkness, while the mountain was burning with fire, you approached me, all the heads of your tribes and your elders;
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when ye heard the voice out of the midst of the darkness, the mountain also burning with fire, then drew ye near unto me, even all the heads of your tribes and your elders,
text.KJV: 5:23 And it came to passe when yee heard the voice out of the midst of the darkenes (for the mountaine did burne with fire) that ye came neere vnto mee, euen all the heads of your tribes, and your elders.
text.DRV: 5:23 And you after you heard the voice out of the midst of the darkness, and saw the mount burn, came to me all the Princes of the tribes and the elders, and you said:

text.NRSV: and you said, “Look, the Lord our God has shown us his glory and greatness, and we have heard his voice out of the fire. Today we have seen that God may speak to someone and the person may still live.
text.Rotherham: and ye said—Lo! Yahweh our God hath let us see his glory and his greatness, his voice also, have we heard out of the midst of the fire,—this day, have we seen, that God may speak with man who yet may live.
text.KJV: 5:24 And ye said, Behold, the Lord our God hath shewed vs his glory, and his greatnesse, and we haue heard his voice out of the midst of the fire: wee haue seene this day that God doth talke with man, and he liueth.
text.DRV: 5:24 Behold the Lord our God hath shewed us his majesty and greatness for we have heard his voice out of the midst of the fire, & have proved this day that God speaking with man, man hath lived.

text.NRSV: So now why should we die? For this great fire will consume us; if we hear the voice of the Lord our God any longer, we shall die.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore why should we die? for this great fire, will consume, us,—if we ourselves, hear the voice of Yahweh our God any more, we shall die.
text.KJV: 5:25 Now therefore why should wee die? for this great fire will consume vs. If we heare the voyce of the Lord our God any more, then we shall die.
text.DRV: 5:25 Why shall we die therefore, and this exceeding great fire devour us? For if we hear the voice of the Lord our God any more, we shall die.

text.NRSV: For who is there of all flesh that has heard the voice of the living God speaking out of fire, as we have, and remained alive?
text.Rotherham: For who [is there] of all flesh, that ever heard the voice of a Living God speaking out of the midst of fire as we, and yet lived?
text.KJV: 5:26 For who is there of all flesh that hath heard the voice of the liuing God, speaking out of the midst of the fire (as we haue) and liued?
text.DRV: 5:26 What is all flesh, that it should hear the voice of the living God, who speaketh out of the midst of the fire as we have heard, and may live?

text.NRSV: Go near, you yourself, and hear all that the Lord our God will say. Then tell us everything that the Lord our God tells you, and we will listen and do it.”
text.Rotherham: Go, thou, near, and hear, all that Yahweh our God shall say,—so shalt thou speak unto us, all that Yahweh our God shall speak unto thee and we will hear and do.
text.KJV: 5:27 Goe thou neere, and heare all that the Lord our God shall say; and speake thou vnto vs all that the Lord our God shall speake vnto thee, and we will heare it, and doe it.
text.DRV: 5:27 Approach thou rather: and hear all things that the Lord our God shall say to thee: and thou shalt speak to us, and we hearing will do them.

text.NRSV: The Lord heard your words when you spoke to me, and the Lord said to me: “I have heard the words of this people, which they have spoken to you; they are right in all that they have spoken.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh heard the voice of your words, when ye spake unto me,—and Yahweh said unto me—I have heard the voice of the words of this people which they have spoken unto thee, they have well said all which they have spoken.
text.KJV: 5:28 And the Lord heard the voice of your words, when ye spake vnto me, and the Lord said vnto me, I haue heard the voice of the wordes of this people, which they haue spoken vnto thee: they haue well said, all that they haue spoken.
text.DRV: 5:28 Which when our Lord had heard, he said to me: I have heard the voice of the words of this people, which they spake to thee: they have spoken all things well.

text.NRSV: If only they had such a mind as this, to fear me and to keep all my commandments always, so that it might go well with them and with their children forever!
text.Rotherham: Oh that this their heart might remain in them to revere me, and to keep all my commandments all the days,—that it might be well with them and with their sons unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 5:29 O that there were such an heart in them, that they would feare me, and keepe my commandements alwayes, that it might bee well with them, and with their children for euer.
text.DRV: 5:29 Who shall give them to have such a mind, that they would fear me, and keep all my commandments at all time, that it may be well with them and with their children for ever?

text.NRSV: Go say to them, ‘Return to your tents.’
text.Rotherham: Go, say unto them,—Return ye to your tents.
text.KJV: 5:30 Goe, say to them, Get you into your tents againe.
text.DRV: 5:30 Go and say to them: Return into your tents.

text.NRSV: But you, stand here by me, and I will tell you all the commandments, the statutes and the ordinances, that you shall teach them, so that they may do them in the land that I am giving them to possess.”
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore here stand with me and let me speak unto thee—all the commandment and the statutes and the regulations which thou shalt teach them,—that they may do [them] in the land which I am giving unto them to possess it.
text.KJV: 5:31 But as for thee, stand thou here by me, and I will speake vnto thee all the Commandements, and the Statutes, and the Iudgements, which thou shalt teach them, that they may doe them in the land which I giue them to possesse it.
text.DRV: 5:31 But thou stand here with me, and I will speak to thee all my commandments, and ceremonies, and judgments: which thou shalt teach them, that they may do them in the Land, which I will give them in possession.

text.NRSV: You must therefore be careful to do as the Lord your God has commanded you; you shall not turn to the right or to the left.
text.Rotherham: So then ye must observe to do, as Yahweh your God hath commanded you,—ye must not turn aside to the right hand or to the left.
text.KJV: 5:32 Ye shall obserue to doe therefore, as the Lord your God hath commanded you: you shall not turne aside to the right hand, or to the left.
text.DRV: 5:32 Keep therefore and do the things which our Lord God hath commanded you: you shall not decline neither to the right hand, nor to the left:

text.NRSV: You must follow exactly the path that the Lord your God has commanded you, so that you may live, and that it may go well with you, and that you may live long in the land that you are to possess.
text.Rotherham: In all the way which Yahweh your God hath commanded you, must ye walk,—that ye may live and it be well with you, and that ye may prolong your days in the land, which ye shall possess.
text.KJV: 5:33 You shall walke in all the wayes which the Lord your God hath commanded you, that ye may liue, and that it may be well with you, and that ye may prolong your dayes in the land which ye shall possesse.
text.DRV: 5:33 but the way that the Lord your God hath commanded shall you walk, that you may live, and it may be well with you, and your days may be prolonged in the land of your possession.

text.NRSV: Now this is the commandment—the statutes and the ordinances—that the Lord your God charged me to teach you to observe in the land that you are about to cross into and occupy,
text.Rotherham: This, then is the commandment—[namely] the statutes and the regulations, which Yahweh your God hath commanded to teach you,—that ye may do them in the land whither ye are passing over to possess it:
text.KJV: 6:1 Now these are the Commaundements, the Statutes, & the Iudgements, which the Lord your God commanded to teach you, that ye might doe them in the land whither ye goe to possesse it:
text.DRV: 6:1 These are the precepts, and ceremonies, and judgments, which the Lord your God commanded that I should teach you, and you should do them in the Land, whereunto you pass over to possess it:

text.NRSV: so that you and your children and your children’s children, may fear the Lord your God all the days of your life, and keep all his decrees and his commandments that I am commanding you, so that your days may be long.
text.Rotherham: that thou mayest revere Yahweh thy God, [so as] to keep all his statutes and his commandments which I am commanding thee, thou and thy son and thy sons’ son, all the days of thy life,—and that thy days may be prolonged.
text.KJV: 6:2 That thou mightest feare the Lord thy God, to keepe all his Statutes, and his Commandements which I command thee; thou, and thy sonne, and thy sonnes sonne, all the dayes of thy life: and that thy dayes may be prolonged.
text.DRV: 6:2 that thou mayst fear the Lord thy God, and keep his commandments and precepts, which I command thee, and thy sons, and nephews, all the days of thy life, that thy days may be prolonged.

text.NRSV: Hear therefore, O Israel, and observe them diligently, so that it may go well with you, and so that you may multiply greatly in a land flowing with milk and honey, as the Lord, the God of your ancestors, has promised you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou hear, O Israel, and shalt observe to do, that it may be well with thee, and that ye may multiply greatly, as Yahweh the God of thy fathers hath spoken to thee, in a land flowing with milk and honey.
text.KJV: 6:3 ¶ Heare therefore, O Israel, and obserue to do it, that it may be wel with thee, and that ye may increase mightily, as the Lord God of thy fathers hath promised thee, in the land that floweth with milke and hony.
text.DRV: 6:3 Hear Israel, and observe that thou do the things which our Lord hath commanded thee, and it may be well with thee, and thou mayst be greatly multiplied, as the Lord God of thy fathers hath promised thee a land flowing with milk & honey.

text.NRSV: Hear, O Israel: The Lord is our God, the Lord alone.
text.Rotherham: Hear, O Israel: Yahweh, is our God,—Yahweh alone.
text.KJV: 6:4 Heare, O Israel, the Lord our God is one Lord.
text.DRV: 6:4 Hear Israel, The Lord our God, is one Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your might.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt therefore love Yahweh thy God,—with all thy heart and with all thy soul, and with all thy might;
text.KJV: 6:5 And thou shalt loue the Lord thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soule, and with all thy might.
text.DRV: 6:5 Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with thy whole strength.

text.NRSV: Keep these words that I am commanding you today in your heart.
text.Rotherham: so shall these words which I am commanding thee to-day, be upon thy heart;
text.KJV: 6:6 And these words which I command thee this day, shall bee in thine heart.
text.DRV: 6:6 And these words, which I command thee this day, shall be in thy heart:

text.NRSV: Recite them to your children and talk about them when you are at home and when you are away, when you lie down and when you rise.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt impress them upon thy sons, and shalt speak of them,—when thou sittest in thy house and when thou walkest by the way, and when thou liest down and when thou risest up;
text.KJV: 6:7 And thou shalt teach them diligently vnto thy children, and shalt talke of them when thou sittest in thine house, and when thou walkest by the way, and when thou liest downe, and when thou risest vp.
text.DRV: 6:7 and thou shalt tell them to thy children, and thou shalt meditate sitting in thy house, and walking on thy journey, sleeping, and rising.

text.NRSV: Bind them as a sign on your hand, fix them as an emblem on your forehead,
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt bind them for a sign upon thy hand,—and they shall serve for bands between thine eyes;
text.KJV: 6:8 And thou shalt binde them for a signe vpon thine hand, and they shalbe as frontlets betweene thine eyes.
text.DRV: 6:8 And thou shalt bind them as a sign on thy hand, and they shall be and shall move between thine eyes,

text.NRSV: and write them on the doorposts of your house and on your gates.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt write them upon the posts of thy house and within thy gates.
text.KJV: 6:9 And thou shalt write them vpon the posts of thy house, and on thy gates.
text.DRV: 6:9 and thou shalt write them in the entry, and on the doors of thy house.

text.NRSV: When the Lord your God has brought you into the land that he swore to your ancestors, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give you—a land with fine, large cities that you did not build,
text.Rotherham: So shall it be, when Yahweh thy God shall bring thee into the land, which he sware to thy fathers—to Abraham to Isaac and to Jacob to give unto thee,—into cities great and goodly, which thou didst not build;
text.KJV: 6:10 And it shall be when the Lord thy God shall haue brought thee into the land which hee sware vnto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Iacob to giue thee, great and goodly cities, which thou buildedst not,
text.DRV: 6:10 And when the Lord thy God shall have brought thee into the Land, for the which he sware to thy fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: and shall have given thee great and goodly cities, which thou didst not build,

text.NRSV: houses filled with all sorts of goods that you did not fill, hewn cisterns that you did not hew, vineyards and olive groves that you did not plant—and when you have eaten your fill,
text.Rotherham: and houses full of all good things, which thou filledst not, and hewn cisterns which thou hewedst not, vineyards and oliveyards which thou plantedst not,—and shalt eat and be full,
text.KJV: 6:11 And houses full of all good things which thou filledst not, and welles digged which thou diggedst not, vineyards and oliue trees which thou plantedst not, when thou shalt haue eaten and be full,
text.DRV: 6:11 houses full of all riches, which thou didst not erect, cisterns which thou didst not dig, vineyards and oliveyards, which thou didst not plant,

text.NRSV: take care that you do not forget the Lord, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
text.Rotherham: take heed unto thyself, lest thou forget Yahweh,—who brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt out of the house of servants.
text.KJV: 6:12 Then beware lest thou forget the Lord which brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage.
text.DRV: 6:12 and thou shalt have eaten and be full:

text.NRSV: The Lord your God you shall fear; him you shall serve, and by his name alone you shall swear.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh thy God, shalt thou revere And him, shalt thou serve,—And by his name, shalt thou swear.
text.KJV: 6:13 Thou shalt feare the Lord thy God, and serue him, & shalt sweare by his Name.
text.DRV: 6:13 take heed diligently lest thou forget our Lord, that brought thee out of the Land of Ægypt, out of the house of servitude. Thou shalt fear the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve, and by his name shalt thou swear.

text.NRSV: Do not follow other gods, any of the gods of the peoples who are all around you,
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not go after other gods,—of the gods of the peoples who are surrounding you;
text.KJV: 6:14 Yee shall not goe after other gods, of the gods of the people which are round about you:
text.DRV: 6:14 You shall not go after the strange gods of all Nations, that are round about you:

text.NRSV: because the Lord your God, who is present with you, is a jealous God. The anger of the Lord your God would be kindled against you and he would destroy you from the face of the earth.
text.Rotherham: for a jealous GOD, is Yahweh thy God in thy midst,—lest the anger of Yahweh thy God kindle upon thee, and so he destroy thee from off the face of the ground.
text.KJV: 6:15 (For the Lord thy God is a ielous God among you) lest the anger of the Lord thy God bee kindled against thee, and destroy thee from off the face of the earth.
text.DRV: 6:15 because the Lord thy God is a Jealous God in the midst of thee: lest sometime the fury of the Lord thy God be wrath against thee, & take thee away from the face of the earth.

text.NRSV: Do not put the Lord your God to the test, as you tested him at Massah.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not put Yahweh your God to the proof,—as ye put him to the proof in Massah [that is, "The place of proving"] .
text.KJV: 6:16 ¶ Ye shall not tempt the Lord your God, as yee tempted him in Massah.
text.DRV: 6:16 Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God, as thou didst tempt him in the place of tentation.

text.NRSV: You must diligently keep the commandments of the Lord your God, and his decrees, and his statutes that he has commanded you.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall, keep, the commandments of Yahweh your God and his testimonies and his statutes which he hath commanded thee;
text.KJV: 6:17 You shall diligently keepe the Commandements of the Lord your God, and his Testimonies, and his Statutes, which he hath commanded thee.
text.DRV: 6:17 Keep the precepts of the Lord thy God, and the testimonies & ceremonies, which he hath commanded thee:

text.NRSV: Do what is right and good in the sight of the Lord , so that it may go well with you, and so that you may go in and occupy the good land that the Lord swore to your ancestors to give you,
text.Rotherham: so shalt thou do what is right and what is pleasing in the eyes of Yahweh,—that it may be well with thee, and thou mayest enter in and possess the good land, which Yahweh sware unto thy fathers;
text.KJV: 6:18 And thou shalt doe that which is right and good in the sight of the Lord: that it may be well with thee, and that thou mayest goe in, and possesse the good land which the Lord sware vnto thy fathers:
text.DRV: 6:18 And do that which is pleasant and good in the sight of our Lord, that it may be well with thee: and entering in thou mayest possess the goodly Land, whereof our Lord sware to thy fathers,

text.NRSV: thrusting out all your enemies from before you, as the Lord has promised.
text.Rotherham: to thrust out all thine enemies from before thee,—As spake Yahweh.
text.KJV: 6:19 To cast out all thine enemies from before thee, as the Lord hath spoken.
text.DRV: 6:19 that he would destroy all thy enemies before thee, as he hath spoken.

text.NRSV: When your children ask you in time to come, “What is the meaning of the decrees and the statutes and the ordinances that the Lord our God has commanded you?”
text.Rotherham: When thy son shall ask thee in time to come, saying,—What mean the testimonies and the statutes and the regulations, which Yahweh our God commanded you?
text.KJV: 6:20 And when thy sonne asketh thee in time to come, saying, What meane the Testimonies, & the Statutes, and the Iudgements, which the Lord our God hath commanded you?
text.DRV: 6:20 And when thy son shall ask thee tomorrow, saying: What mean these testimonies, and ceremonies, and judgments, which the Lord our God hath commanded us?

text.NRSV: then you shall say to your children, “We were Pharaoh’s slaves in Egypt, but the Lord brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say unto thy son, Servants, were we unto Pharaoh in Egypt,—and Yahweh brought us forth out of Egypt with a firm hand;
text.KJV: 6:21 Then thou shalt say vnto thy sonne, We were Pharaohs bondmen in Egypt, and the Lord brought vs out of Egypt with a mighty hand.
text.DRV: 6:21 thou shalt say to him: We were the bondmen of Pharao in Ægypt, and our Lord brought us out of Ægypt in a strong hand:

text.NRSV: The Lord displayed before our eyes great and awesome signs and wonders against Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his household.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh put forth signs and wonders great and sore upon Egypt upon Pharaoh and upon all his household before our eyes;
text.KJV: 6:22 And the Lord shewed signes and wonders, great and sore vpon Egypt, vpon Pharaoh, and vpon all his houshold, before our eyes:
text.DRV: 6:22 and he did signs & wonders great and very sore in Ægypt against Pharao, and all his house, in our sight,

text.NRSV: He brought us out from there in order to bring us in, to give us the land that he promised on oath to our ancestors.
text.Rotherham: but he brought us forth from thence,—that he might bring us in, to give unto us the land, which he sware unto our fathers.
text.KJV: 6:23 And hee brought vs out from thence, that hee might bring vs in, to giue vs the land which hee sware vnto our fathers.
text.DRV: 6:23 and he brought us out from thence, that being brought in he might give us the Land, whereupon he sware to our fathers.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord commanded us to observe all these statutes, to fear the Lord our God, for our lasting good, so as to keep us alive, as is now the case.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh commanded us to do all these statutes, to revere Yahweh our God,—for our good, all the days, to preserve us alive (as at this day);
text.KJV: 6:24 And the Lord commanded vs to doe all these Statutes, to feare the Lord our God, for our good alwayes, that he might preserue vs aliue, as it is at this day.
text.DRV: 6:24 And our Lord commanded that we should do all these ordinances, and should fear the Lord our God, that it might be well with us all the days of our life, as it is at this day.

text.NRSV: If we diligently observe this entire commandment before the Lord our God, as he has commanded us, we will be in the right.”
text.Rotherham: and righteousness, shall it be to us,—when we take heed to do all this commandment, before Yahweh our God, as he commanded us.
text.KJV: 6:25 And it shall be our righteousnes, if we obserue to doe all these Commandements, before the Lord our God, as he hath commanded vs.
text.DRV: 6:25 And he will be merciful to us, if we keep and do all his precepts before the Lord our God, as he commanded us.

text.NRSV: When the Lord your God brings you into the land that you are about to enter and occupy, and he clears away many nations before you—the Hittites, the Girgashites, the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, seven nations mightier and more numerous than you—
text.Rotherham: When Yahweh thy God shall bring thee into the land which thou art going in to possess, and shall clear away many nations from before thee—the Hittites and the Girgashites and the Amorites, and the Canaanites and the Perizzites, and the Hivites and the Jebusites—seven nations greater in number and stronger than thou;
text.KJV: 7:1 When the Lord thy God shall bring thee into the land whither thou goest to possesse it, and hath cast out many nations before thee, the Hittites, and the Girgashites, and the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Perizzites, and the Hiuites, and the Iebusites, seuen nations greater and mightier then thou:
text.DRV: 7:1 When the Lord thy God shall have brought thee into the land, which thou doest enter in to possess, and shall have destroyed many Nations before thee, the Hetheite, and the Gergezeite, and the Amorrhite, and the Chananite, and the Pherezite, and the Heveite, and the Jebusite, seven nations of much greater number than thou art, and stronger than thou:

text.NRSV: and when the Lord your God gives them over to you and you defeat them, then you must utterly destroy them. Make no covenant with them and show them no mercy.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh thy God shall deliver them up before thee, and thou shalt smite them, thou shalt devote them to destruction, thou shalt not solemnise with them a covenant neither shalt thou shew them favour;
text.KJV: 7:2 And when the Lord thy God shall deliuer them before thee, thou shalt smite them, and vtterly destroy them, thou shalt make no couenant with them, nor shew mercy vnto them.
text.DRV: 7:2 and the Lord thy God shall have delivered them to thee, thou shalt strike them unto utter destruction. Thou shalt not make league with them, nor pity them,

text.NRSV: Do not intermarry with them, giving your daughters to their sons or taking their daughters for your sons,
text.Rotherham: neither shalt thou intermarry with them,—thy daughter, shalt thou not give unto his son, nor his daughter, shalt thou take for thy son;
text.KJV: 7:3 Neither shalt thou make marriages with them: thy daughter thou shalt not giue vnto his sonne, nor his daughter shalt thou take vnto thy sonne.
text.DRV: 7:3 nor make marriages with them. Thy daughter thou shalt not give to his son, nor take his daughter for thy son:

text.NRSV: for that would turn away your children from following me, to serve other gods. Then the anger of the Lord would be kindled against you, and he would destroy you quickly.
text.Rotherham: for he will turn aside thy son from following me, and they will serve other gods,—so shall the anger of Yahweh kindle upon you, and he will destroy thee, speedily.
text.KJV: 7:4 For they will turne away thy sonne from following mee, that they may serue other gods: so will the anger of the Lord be kindled against you, and destroy thee suddenly.
text.DRV: 7:4 for he will seduce thy son, that he follow not me, and that he rather serve strange gods, and the fury of our Lord will be wrath, and shall quickly destroy thee.

text.NRSV: But this is how you must deal with them: break down their altars, smash their pillars, hew down their sacred poles, and burn their idols with fire.
text.Rotherham: Verily, thus, shall ye do unto them: Their altars, shall ye tear down, And their pillars, shall ye break in pieces,—And their sacred stems, shall ye hew down, And their carved images, shall ye burn up in the fire.
text.KJV: 7:5 But thus shal ye deale with them; ye shall destroy their altars, and breake downe their images, and cut downe their groues, and burne their grauen images with fire.
text.DRV: 7:5 But these things rather you shall do to them: Overthrow their altars, and break their statues, and cut down their groves, and burn their sculptiles.

text.NRSV: For you are a people holy to the Lord your God; the Lord your God has chosen you out of all the peoples on earth to be his people, his treasured possession.
text.Rotherham: For a holy people, art thou unto Yahweh thy God: of thee, hath Yahweh thy God, made choice, that thou shouldest become his people as a treasure, above all the peoples that are on the face of the ground:
text.KJV: 7:6 For thou art an holy people vnto the Lord thy God: the Lord thy God hath chosen thee to be a special people vnto himselfe, aboue all people that are vpon the face of the earth.
text.DRV: 7:6 Because thou art a holy people to the Lord thy God. The Lord thy God hath chosen thee, to be his peculiar people of all peoples, that are upon the earth.

text.NRSV: It was not because you were more numerous than any other people that the Lord set his heart on you and chose you—for you were the fewest of all peoples.
text.Rotherham: not because ye were more in number than any of the peoples, did Yahweh become attached to you and make choice of you,—for, ye, were fewer than any of the peoples;
text.KJV: 7:7 The Lord did not set his loue vpon you, nor choose you, because yee were moe in number then any people: (for ye were the fewest of all people,)
text.DRV: 7:7 Not because you passed all nations in number, is our Lord joined unto you, & hath chosen you, whereas you are fewer than all peoples:

text.NRSV: It was because the Lord loved you and kept the oath that he swore to your ancestors, that the Lord has brought you out with a mighty hand, and redeemed you from the house of slavery, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: but because Yahweh loved you and because he would keep the oath which he sware unto your fathers, did Yahweh bring you forth with a firm hand,—and redeem thee out of the house of servants, out of the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.
text.KJV: 7:8 But because the Lord loued you, and because hee would keepe the othe which hee had sworne vnto your fathers, hath the Lord brought you out with a mighty hand, and redeemed you out of the house of bondmen, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.
text.DRV: 7:8 but because our Lord hath loved you, and hath kept the oath, which he sware to your fathers: and hath brought you forth in a strong hand, and redeemed you from the house of servitude, out of the hand of Pharao the king of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Know therefore that the Lord your God is God, the faithful God who maintains covenant loyalty with those who love him and keep his commandments, to a thousand generations,
text.Rotherham: So then, thou must know that, Yahweh thy God, he, is God,—the faithful GOD, keeping his covenant and his lovingkindness with them that love him and keep his commandments, to a thousand generations
text.KJV: 7:9 Know therefore that the Lord thy God, he is God, the faithfull God, which keepeth Couenant and Mercy with them that loue him, and keepe his Commandements, to a thousand generations;
text.DRV: 7:9 And thou shalt know that the Lord thy God, he is a strong and faithful God, keeping his covenant and mercy to them that love him, and to them that keep his precepts, unto a thousand generations:

text.NRSV: and who repays in their own person those who reject him. He does not delay but repays in their own person those who reject him.
text.Rotherham: but requiting them who hate him unto his face to destroy them,—he will not be slack towards him that hateth him, unto his face, will he requite him.
text.KJV: 7:10 And repaieth them that hate him to their face, to destroy them: he wil not be slacke to him that hateth him, he will repay him to his face.
text.DRV: 7:10 and rendering forthwith to them that hate him, so that he destroyeth them, and deferreth no longer, immediately rendering to them that they deserve.

text.NRSV: Therefore, observe diligently the commandment—the statutes, and the ordinances—that I am commanding you today.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou keep the commandment and the statutes and the regulations, which I am commanding thee to-day, to do them.
text.KJV: 7:11 Thou shalt therefore keepe the Commandements, and the Statutes, and the Iudgements, which I command thee this day, to doe them.
text.DRV: 7:11 Keep therefore the precepts and ceremonies and judgments, which I command thee this day to do them.

text.NRSV: If you heed these ordinances, by diligently observing them, the Lord your God will maintain with you the covenant loyalty that he swore to your ancestors;
text.Rotherham: Then shall it come to pass if ye will hearken unto these regulations, and keep and do them, then will Yahweh thy God keep with thee the covenant and the lovingkindness which he sware unto thy fathers;
text.KJV: 7:12 ¶ Wherefore it shal come to passe, if ye hearken to these iudgements, and keepe and do them: That the Lord thy God shall keepe vnto thee the Couenant and the Mercy which he sware vnto thy fathers.
text.DRV: 7:12 If after thou hast heard these judgments, thou keep and do them, the Lord also thy God will keep the covenant unto thee, and the mercy which he sware to thy fathers:

text.NRSV: he will love you, bless you, and multiply you; he will bless the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground, your grain and your wine and your oil, the increase of your cattle and the issue of your flock, in the land that he swore to your ancestors to give you.
text.Rotherham: and will love thee, and bless thee and multiply thee,—and bless the fruit of thy body and the fruit of thy ground thy corn and thy new wine and thine oil the young of thy kine and the ewes of thy flock, upon the soil which he swart unto thy fathers to give unto thee.
text.KJV: 7:13 And hee will loue thee, and blesse thee, and multiply thee: Hee will also blesse the fruit of thy wombe, and the fruit of thy land, thy corne, and thy wine, and thine oile, the encrease of thy kine, and the flockes of thy sheepe, in the land which hee sware vnto thy fathers to giue thee.
text.DRV: 7:13 and he will love and multiply thee, and will bless the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy land, thy corn, and vintage, oil, and herds, the flocks of thy sheep upon the Land, for the which he sware to thy fathers that he would give it thee.

text.NRSV: You shall be the most blessed of peoples, with neither sterility nor barrenness among you or your livestock.
text.Rotherham: Blessed shalt thou be beyond all the peoples,—there shall not be in thee a barren male or female, nor among thy cattle;
text.KJV: 7:14 Thou shalt bee blessed aboue all people: there shall not bee male or female barren among you or among your cattell.
text.DRV: 7:14 Blessed shalt thou be among all peoples. There shall be none barren with thee of neither sex, as well in men as in thy flocks.

text.NRSV: The Lord will turn away from you every illness; all the dread diseases of Egypt that you experienced, he will not inflict on you, but he will lay them on all who hate you.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh, will turn away, from thee, all disease,—and as for all the sore sicknesses of Egypt which thou knowest, he will not lay them upon thee, but will put them upon all who hate thee;
text.KJV: 7:15 And the Lord will take away from thee all sickenesse, and will put none of the euill diseases of Egypt (which thou knowest) vpon thee: but will lay them vpon all them that hate thee.
text.DRV: 7:15 Our Lord will take away from thee all disease: and the sore infirmities of Ægypt, which thou knowest, he will not bring upon thee, but upon all thine enemies.

text.NRSV: You shall devour all the peoples that the Lord your God is giving over to you, showing them no pity; you shall not serve their gods, for that would be a snare to you.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt devour all the peoples whom, Yahweh thy God, is delivering up unto thee, thine eye shall not look with pity upon them, neither shalt thou serve their gods, for a snare, would it be unto thee.
text.KJV: 7:16 And thou shalt consume all the people which the Lord thy God shall deliuer thee: thine eye shall haue no pitie vpon them, neither shalt thou serue their gods, for that will be a snare vnto thee.
text.DRV: 7:16 Thou shalt devour all the peoples, which the Lord thy God will give thee. Thine eye shall not spare them, neither shalt thou serve their gods, lest they be the ruin of thee.

text.NRSV: If you say to yourself, “These nations are more numerous than I; how can I dispossess them?”
text.Rotherham: Although thou mightest say in thy heart: More in number, are these nations than I,—how can, I dispossess them?
text.KJV: 7:17 If thou shalt say in thine heart, These nations are moe then I, howe can I dispossesse them?
text.DRV: 7:17 If thou say in thy heart: These nations are more than I, how shall I be able to destroy them?

text.NRSV: do not be afraid of them. Just remember what the Lord your God did to Pharaoh and to all Egypt,
text.Rotherham: thou must not be afraid of them,—thou must, remember, what Yahweh thy God did, unto Pharaoh and unto all Egypt:
text.KJV: 7:18 Thou shalt not be afraid of them: but shalt well remember, what the Lord thy God did vnto Pharaoh, and vnto all Egypt,
text.DRV: 7:18 Fear not, but remember what the Lord thy God did to Pharao & to all the Ægyptians,

text.NRSV: the great trials that your eyes saw, the signs and wonders, the mighty hand and the outstretched arm by which the Lord your God brought you out. The Lord your God will do the same to all the peoples of whom you are afraid.
text.Rotherham: the great provings which thine own eyes saw, and the signs and the wonders and the firm hand and the stretched-out arm, wherewith Yahweh thy God brought thee forth,—so, will Yahweh thy God do unto all the peoples, before whom thou art afraid.
text.KJV: 7:19 The great temptations which thine eyes sawe, and the signes and the wonders, and the mightie hand, and the stretched out arme, whereby the Lord thy God brought thee out: so shall the Lord thy God doe vnto all the people of whom thou art afraid.
text.DRV: 7:19 the exceeding great plagues, which thine eyes saw, and the signs and wonders, and the strong hand, and the stretched out arm, that the Lord thy God might bring thee forth: so will he do to all peoples, whom thou fearest.

text.NRSV: Moreover, the Lord your God will send the pestilence against them, until even the survivors and the fugitives are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, the hornet, will Yahweh thy God send among them,—until they are destroyed who are left remaining, and who are hiding themselves from thy face.
text.KJV: 7:20 Moreouer, the Lord thy God will send the hornet among them, vntill they that are left and hide themselues from thee, be destroyed.
text.DRV: 7:20 Moreover hornets also will the Lord thy God send upon them, until he destroy and consume all that escaped thee, & can hide themselves.

text.NRSV: Have no dread of them, for the Lord your God, who is present with you, is a great and awesome God.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not be affrighted at their presence,—for Yahweh thy God in thy midst is a Go great and to be revered.
text.KJV: 7:21 Thou shalt not bee affrighted at them: for the Lord thy God is among you, a mightie God, and terrible.
text.DRV: 7:21 Thou shalt not fear them, because the Lord thy God is in the midst of thee, a mighty God & terrible:

text.NRSV: The Lord your God will clear away these nations before you little by little; you will not be able to make a quick end of them, otherwise the wild animals would become too numerous for you.
text.Rotherham: So will Yahweh thy God, clear away these nations from before thee little by little,—thou mayest not consume them at once, lest the wild beast of the field should multiply ever thee.
text.KJV: 7:22 And the Lord thy God will put out those nations before thee by litle and litle: thou mayest not consume them at once, lest the beastes of the field increase vpon thee.
text.DRV: 7:22 he will consume these nations in thy sight by little & little and by parts. Thou mayest not destroy them all together: lest perhaps the beasts of the earth multiply against thee.

text.NRSV: But the Lord your God will give them over to you, and throw them into great panic, until they are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: But Yahweh thy God will deliver them up before thee,—and discomfit them with a great discomfiture, until they are destroyed;
text.KJV: 7:23 But the Lord thy God shall deliuer them vnto thee, and shall destroy them with a mightie destruction, vntill they be destroyed.
text.DRV: 7:23 And the Lord thy God will give them in thy sight: an will kill them until they be utterly destroyed.

text.NRSV: He will hand their kings over to you and you shall blot out their name from under heaven; no one will be able to stand against you, until you have destroyed them.
text.Rotherham: and will deliver their kings into thy hand and thou shalt destroy their name, from under the heavens,—not a man shall stand before thee, until thou hast destroyed them.
text.KJV: 7:24 And he shall deliuer their kings into thine hand, and thou shalt destroy their name from vnder heauen: There shal no man be able to stand before thee, vntil thou haue destroyed them.
text.DRV: 7:24 And he will deliver their kings into thy hands, and thou shalt destroy their names under heaven: no man shall be able to resist thee, until thou destroy them.

text.NRSV: The images of their gods you shall burn with fire. Do not covet the silver or the gold that is on them and take it for yourself, because you could be ensnared by it; for it is abhorrent to the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: The carved images of their gods, shalt thou consume with fire,—thou shalt not covet the silver and gold upon them to take unto thee, lest thou be ensnared thereby; for the abomination of Yahweh thy God, it is;
text.KJV: 7:25 The grauen images of their gods shall yee burne with fire: thou shalt not desire the siluer or golde that is on them, nor take it vnto thee, lest thou bee snared therein: for it is an abomination to the Lord thy God.
text.DRV: 7:25 Their sculptiles thou shalt burn with fire: thou shalt not covet the silver and gold, whereof they were made, neither shalt thou take to thee any thing thereof, lest thou offend, because it is the abomination of the Lord thy God.

text.NRSV: Do not bring an abhorrent thing into your house, or you will be set apart for destruction like it. You must utterly detest and abhor it, for it is set apart for destruction.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt not bring an abomination into thy house, and so become devoted to destruction, like it, thou shalt, abhor, it, and thou shalt, loathe it, because a thing devoted to destruction, it is.
text.KJV: 7:26 Neither shalt thou bring an abomination into thine house, lest thou bee a cursed thing like it: but thou shalt vtterly detest it, and thou shalt vtterly abhorre it, for it is a cursed thing.
text.DRV: 7:26 Neither shalt thou bring in ought of the idol into thy house, lest thou become anathema, as also that is. As filthiness thou shalt detest it, and as uncleanness and filth thou shalt account it abominable, because it is anathema.

text.NRSV: This entire commandment that I command you today you must diligently observe, so that you may live and increase, and go in and occupy the land that the Lord promised on oath to your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: All the commandment which I am commanding thee to-day, shalt thou, observe, to do,—that ye may, live, and multiply, and enter in and possess the land, which Yahweh sware unto your fathers.
text.KJV: 8:1 All the commaundements which I commaund thee this day, shall yee obserue to doe, that yee may liue, and multiply, and goe in, and possesse the land which the Lord sware vnto your fathers.
text.DRV: 8:1 Every commandment, that I command thee this day, take diligent heed that thou do it: that you may live, and be multiplied, and entering in may possess the Land, for the which our Lord sware to your fathers.

text.NRSV: Remember the long way that the Lord your God has led you these forty years in the wilderness, in order to humble you, testing you to know what was in your heart, whether or not you would keep his commandments.
text.Rotherham: So then, thou shalt remember all the way in which Yahweh thy God caused thee to journey these forty years in the desert,—that he might humble thee to put thee to the proof to know what was in thy heart,—whether thou wouldest keep his commandments or not.
text.KJV: 8:2 And thou shalt remember all the way which the Lord thy God led thee these fourtie yeeres in the wildernesse, to humble thee, and to proue thee, to know what was in thine heart, whether thou wouldest keepe his commandements, or no.
text.DRV: 8:2 And thou shalt remember all the journey, through the which the Lord thy God hath brought thee forty years by the desert, that he might afflict & prove thee, & that the things that were in thy heart might be made known, whether thou wouldest keep his commandments or not.

text.NRSV: He humbled you by letting you hunger, then by feeding you with manna, with which neither you nor your ancestors were acquainted, in order to make you understand that one does not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord.
