text.DRV: 31:7 And Moyses called Josue, & said to him before all Israel: Take courage, & be strong: for thou shalt bring in this people into the Land, which our Lord sware that he would give to their fathers, and thou shalt divide it by lot.

text.NRSV: It is the Lord who goes before you. He will be with you; he will not fail you or forsake you. Do not fear or be dismayed.”
text.Rotherham: But, Yahweh, it is who is going before thee, he will be with thee, he will not fail thee nor forsake thee,—thou must not fear nor be dismayed.
text.KJV: 31:8 And the Lord, he it is that doth goe before thee, he will be with thee, hee will not faile thee, neither forsake thee: feare not, neither be dismayed.
text.DRV: 31:8 And our Lord that is your conductor, himself will be with thee: he will not leave, nor forsake thee: fear not, neither dread thou.

text.NRSV: Then Moses wrote down this law, and gave it to the priests, the sons of Levi, who carried the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and to all the elders of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Moses wrote this law, and delivered it unto the priests the sons of Levi, who were bearing the ark of the covenant of Yahweh,—and unto all the elders of Israel.
text.KJV: 31:9 ¶ And Moses wrote this Law, and deliuered it vnto the Priests the sonnes of Leui, which bare the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord, and vnto all the Elders of Israel.
text.DRV: 31:9 Moyses therefore wrote this law, & delivered it to the Priests the sons of Levi, which carried the ark of the covenant of our Lord, & to all the ancients of Israel.

text.NRSV: Moses commanded them: “Every seventh year, in the scheduled year of remission, during the festival of booths,
text.Rotherham: And Moses commanded them, saying,—At the end of seven years in the appointed season of the year of release, during the festival of booths;
text.KJV: 31:10 And Moses commanded them, saying, At the end of euery seuen yeeres, in the solemnitie of the yeere of release, in the feast of Tabernacles,
text.DRV: 31:10 And he commanded them saying: After seven years, in the year of remission, in the solemnity of tabernacles,

text.NRSV: when all Israel comes to appear before the Lord your God at the place that he will choose, you shall read this law before all Israel in their hearing.
text.Rotherham: when all Israel cometh in to see the face of Yahweh thy God, in the place which he shall choose, thou shalt read this law before all Israel, in their hearing.
text.KJV: 31:11 When all Israel is come to appeare before the Lord thy God, in the place which hee shall choose; thou shalt reade this Law before all Israel, in their hearing.
text.DRV: 31:11 when all come together out of Israel, to appear in the sight of our Lord thy God, in the place which our Lord shall choose, thou shalt read the words of this law before all Israel, they hearing,

text.NRSV: Assemble the people—men, women, and children, as well as the aliens residing in your towns—so that they may hear and learn to fear the Lord your God and to observe diligently all the words of this law,
text.Rotherham: Call together the people—the men and the women and the little ones, and thy sojourner who is within thy gates,—that they may hear, and that they may learn, so shall they revere Yahweh your God, and observe to do all the words of this law;
text.KJV: 31:12 Gather the people together, men, and women and children, and thy stranger that is within thy gates, that they may heare, and that they may learne, and feare the Lord your God, and obserue to doe all the wordes of this Law:
text.DRV: 31:12 and the people being assembled together, as well men as women, children, & strangers, that are within thy gates: that hearing they may learn, and fear our Lord your God, and keep, and fulfill all the words of this law.

text.NRSV: and so that their children, who have not known it, may hear and learn to fear the Lord your God, as long as you live in the land that you are crossing over the Jordan to possess.”
text.Rotherham: and that their children who know not, may hear and learn, that they may revere Yahweh your God,—all the days that ye are living upon the soil, which ye are passing over the Jordan to possess.
text.KJV: 31:13 And that their children which haue not knowen any thing, may heare, and learne to feare the Lord your God, as long as yee liue in the land, whither ye goe ouer Iordan to possesse it.
text.DRV: 31:13 Their children also who now are ignorant; that they may hear, and fear our Lord their God, all the days that they live in the Land, which passing over Jordan you go to obtain.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Your time to die is near; call Joshua and present yourselves in the tent of meeting, so that I may commission him.” So Moses and Joshua went and presented themselves in the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses: Lo! thy days have drawn near that thou must die. Call Joshua and station yourselves in the tent of meeting, that I may command him. So Moses went, with Joshua, and they took their station in the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 31:14 ¶ And the Lord saide vnto Moses, Beholde, thy dayes approach that thou must die: call Ioshua, and present your selues in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, that I may giue him a charge. And Moses and Ioshua went and presented themselues in the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.DRV: 31:14 And our Lord said to Moyses: Behold the days of thy death are nigh: call Josue, and stand ye in the tabernacle of testimony, that I may command him. Moyses therefore and Josue went, & stood in the tabernacle of testimony,

text.NRSV: and the Lord appeared at the tent in a pillar of cloud; the pillar of cloud stood at the entrance to the tent.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh appeared in the tent, in a pillar of cloud,—and the pillar of cloud stood, at the entrance of the tent.
text.KJV: 31:15 And the Lord appeared in the Tabernacle in a pillar of a cloud: and the pillar of the cloude stood ouer the doore of the Tabernacle.
text.DRV: 31:15 and our Lord appeared there in the pillar of a cloud, which stood in the entering of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, “Soon you will lie down with your ancestors. Then this people will begin to prostitute themselves to the foreign gods in their midst, the gods of the land into which they are going; they will forsake me, breaking my covenant that I have made with them.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Lo! thou art about to sleep with thy fathers,—and this people will rise up, and go away unchastely after the gods of the foreigners of the land into the midst of whom they are entering, and will forsake me and break my covenant which I solemnised with them.
text.KJV: 31:16 ¶ And the Lord saide vnto Moses, Behold, thou shalt sleepe with thy fathers, and this people wil rise vp, and goe a whoring after the gods of the strangers of the land whither they goe to be amongst them, and wil forsake me, and breake my couenant which I haue made with them.
text.DRV: 31:16 And our Lord said to Moyses: Behold thou shalt sleep with thy fathers, and this people rising up will fornicate after strange gods in the Land, to the which it entereth to dwell therein: there will they forsake me, and will make the covenant, which I have made with them, of none effect.

text.NRSV: My anger will be kindled against them in that day. I will forsake them and hide my face from them; they will become easy prey, and many terrible troubles will come upon them. In that day they will say, ‘Have not these troubles come upon us because our God is not in our midst?’
text.Rotherham: Then will mine anger kindle upon them in that day, and I will forsake them and will hide my face from them and they shall be consumed, and many calamities and misfortunes shall find them out,—and they will say, in that day, Is it not because my God is not in my midst, that these calamities have found me out?
text.KJV: 31:17 Then my anger shall be kindled against them in that day, and I will forsake them, and I will hide my face from them, and they shall be deuoured, and many euils and troubles shall befall them, so that they will say in that day, Are not these euils come vpon vs, because our God is not amongst vs?
text.DRV: 31:17 And my fury shall be wrath against them in that day, & I will forsake them, and will hide my face from them, and they shall be devoured: all evils and afflictions shall find them, so that they shall say in that day: In truth because God is not with me, these evils have found me.

text.NRSV: On that day I will surely hide my face on account of all the evil they have done by turning to other gods.
text.Rotherham: But, I, will utterly, hide, my face, in that day, because of all the wickedness which they have done, in that they have turned away unto other gods.
text.KJV: 31:18 And I will surely hide my face in that day, for all the euils which they shal haue wrought, in that they are turned vnto other gods.
text.DRV: 31:18 But I will hide, and keep close my face in that day, for all the evils which they have done, because they have followed strange gods.

text.NRSV: Now therefore write this song, and teach it to the Israelites; put it in their mouths, in order that this song may be a witness for me against the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore write ye for you this Song, and teach it unto the sons of Israel put it in their mouths,—that this Song may become for me a witness against the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 31:19 Now therefore, write ye this song for you, and teach it the children of Israel: put it in their mouthes, that this song may be a witnesse for mee, against the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 31:19 Now therefore write unto you this canticle, & teach the children of Israel: that they know it by heart, and sing it by mouth, & this song be unto me for a testimony among the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: For when I have brought them into the land flowing with milk and honey, which I promised on oath to their ancestors, and they have eaten their fill and grown fat, they will turn to other gods and serve them, despising me and breaking my covenant.
text.Rotherham: When I bring them upon the soil which I sware unto their fathers, flowing with milk and honey, and they shall eat and be filled and wax fat, then will they turn away unto other gods and serve them, and despise me, and break my covenant.
text.KJV: 31:20 For when I shall haue brought them into the land which I sware vnto their fathers, that floweth with milke and hony; and they shall haue eaten and filled themselues, and waxen fat; then will they turne vnto other gods, and serue them, and prouoke me, and breake my couenant.
text.DRV: 31:20 For I will bring them into the Land, for the which I sware to their fathers, flowing with milk and honey: And when they have eaten, and are full, and fat, they will turn away to strange gods, and serve them: and will detract from me, and make my covenant of none effect.

text.NRSV: And when many terrible troubles come upon them, this song will confront them as a witness, because it will not be lost from the mouths of their descendants. For I know what they are inclined to do even now, before I have brought them into the land that I promised them on oath.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, when many calamities and misfortunes shall find them out, then shall this Song respond to their face, as a witness, for it shall not be forgotten out of the mouth of their seed, for I know their imagination, which they are forming to-day, ere yet I bring them into the land of which I sware unto their fathers.
text.KJV: 31:21 And it shall come to passe, when many euils and troubles are befallen them, that this song shall testifie against them as a witnesse: for it shall not bee forgotten out of the mouthes of their seed: for I know their imagination which they goe about euen now, before I haue brought them into the land, which I sware.
text.DRV: 31:21 After that many evils and afflictions shall have found them, this canticle shall answer them for a testimony, which no oblivion shall take away out of the mouth of their seed. For I know their cogitations, what things they are about to do this day, before that I bring them into the Land which I have promised them.

text.NRSV: That very day Moses wrote this song and taught it to the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Moses therefore wrote this Song on that day,—and taught it unto the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 31:22 ¶ Moses therefore wrote this song the same day, and taught it the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 31:22 Moyses therefore wrote the canticle and taught it the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: Then the Lord commissioned Joshua son of Nun and said, “Be strong and bold, for you shall bring the Israelites into the land that I promised them; I will be with you.”
text.Rotherham: And he commanded Joshua, son of Nun, and said—Be firm and bold, for, thou shalt bring the sons of Israel into the land which I have sworn to them,—and, I, will be with thee.
text.KJV: 31:23 And he gaue Ioshua the sonne of Nun a charge, and said, Bee strong, and of a good courage: for thou shalt bring the children of Israel into the land which I sware vnto them: and I will be with thee.
text.DRV: 31:23 And our Lord commanded Josue the son of Nun, and said: Take courage, and be strong: for thou shalt bring the children of Israel into the Land which I have promised, and I will be with thee.

text.NRSV: When Moses had finished writing down in a book the words of this law to the very end,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Moses had made an end of writing the words of this law, upon a scroll,—until he had finished them,
text.KJV: 31:24 ¶ And it came to passe when Moses had made an ende of writing the wordes of this Law in a booke, vntill they were finished,
text.DRV: 31:24 Therefore after that Moyses wrote the words of this law in a volume, and finished it,

text.NRSV: Moses commanded the Levites who carried the ark of the covenant of the Lord, saying,
text.Rotherham: then Moses commanded the Levites, who were bearing the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, saying:
text.KJV: 31:25 That Moses commaunded the Leuites which bare the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord, saying,
text.DRV: 31:25 he commanded the Levites, that carried the ark of the covenant of our Lord, saying:

text.NRSV: “Take this book of the law and put it beside the ark of the covenant of the Lord your God; let it remain there as a witness against you.
text.Rotherham: Take this scroll of the law, and put it at the side of the ark of the covenant of Yahweh your God,—so shall it be there in thy midst as a witness.
text.KJV: 31:26 Take this booke of the Law, and put it in the side of the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord your God, that it may bee there for a witnesse against thee.
text.DRV: 31:26 Take this book, and put it in the side of the ark of the covenant of our Lord your God: that it may be for a testimony against thee.

text.NRSV: For I know well how rebellious and stubborn you are. If you already have been so rebellious toward the Lord while I am still alive among you, how much more after my death!
text.Rotherham: For, I, know thy perverseness, and thy stiff neck,—lo! while I am yet alive with you to-day, ye are, quarrelling, with Yahweh, and how much more after my death?
text.KJV: 31:27 For I know thy rebellion, and thy stiffe necke: Beholde, while I am yet aliue with you this day, yee haue bene rebellious against the Lord; and how much more after my death?
text.DRV: 31:27 For I know thy contention, and thy most stiff neck. Whiles I yet live and go in with you, you have done always contentiously against our Lord: how much more when I shall be dead?

text.NRSV: Assemble to me all the elders of your tribes and your officials, so that I may recite these words in their hearing and call heaven and earth to witness against them.
text.Rotherham: Call together unto me all the elders of your tribes and your officers,—and let me speak in their hearing these words, and let me take to witness against them the heavens and the earth.
text.KJV: 31:28 ¶ Gather vnto mee all the Elders of your tribes, and your Officers, that I may speake these words in their eares, and call heauen and earth to record against them.
text.DRV: 31:28 Gather to me all the ancients by your tribes, and your doctors, and I will speak these words in their hearing, and will invocate against them heaven and earth.

text.NRSV: For I know that after my death you will surely act corruptly, turning aside from the way that I have commanded you. In time to come trouble will befall you, because you will do what is evil in the sight of the Lord, provoking him to anger through the work of your hands.”
text.Rotherham: For I know that after my death, ye will break faith, and turn aside out of the way which I have commanded you,—and calamity will befall you in the afterpart of the days, because ye will do the thing that is wicked in the eyes of Yahweh, to provoke him with the work of your own hands.
text.KJV: 31:29 For I know, that after my death yee will vtterly corrupt your selues, and turne aside from the way, which I haue commauded you: and euil wil befall you in the latter dayes, because yee wil doe euil in the sight of the Lord, to prouoke him to anger through the worke of your hands.
text.DRV: 31:29 For I know that after my death you will do wickedly, and will decline quickly from the way, that I have commanded you: and evils shall come upon you in the later times, when you shall do evil in the sight of our Lord, to provoke him by the works of your hands.

text.NRSV: Then Moses recited the words of this song, to the very end, in the hearing of the whole assembly of Israel:
text.Rotherham: So Moses spake in the hearing of all the convocation of Israel the words of this Song, until they were ended:—
text.KJV: 31:30 And Moses spake in the eares of al the Cōgregation of Israel the words of this song, vntill they were ended.
text.DRV: 31:30 Moyses therefore spake in the hearing of the whole assembly of Israel the words of this song, and finished it even to the end.

text.NRSV:
Give ear, O heavens, and I will speak;
	let the earth hear the words of my mouth.
text.Rotherham: Give ear O ye heavens and I will speak, And let the earth hear the sayings of my mouth:
text.KJV: 32:1 Giue eare, O yee heauens, and I will speake; And heare, O earth, the words of my mouth.
text.DRV: 32:1 Hear ye heavens what things I speak, the earth hear the words of my mouth.

text.NRSV:
May my teaching drop like the rain,
	my speech condense like the dew;
like gentle rain on grass,
	like showers on new growth.
text.Rotherham: Let my teaching drop as the rain, Let my speech distil as the dew,—As copious rains on tender grass, As myriad drops on seeding plants.
text.KJV: 32:2 My doctrine shall drop as the raine: my speech shall distill as the deaw, as the smal raine vpon the tender herbe, and as the showres vpon the grasse.
text.DRV: 32:2 My doctrine grow together as rain, my speech flow as the dew, as it were a shower upon the herb, and as it were drops upon the grass.

text.NRSV:
For I will proclaim the name of the Lord;
	ascribe greatness to our God!
text.Rotherham: When, the name of Yahweh, I proclaim, Ascribe ye greatness unto our God:—
text.KJV: 32:3 Because I wil publish the Name of the Lord: ascribe yee greatnesse vnto our God.
text.DRV: 32:3 Because I will invocate the name of our Lord: give magnificence to our God.

text.NRSV:
The Rock, his work is perfect,
	and all his ways are just.
A faithful God, without deceit,
	just and upright is he;
text.Rotherham: A Rock! faultless his work, For, all his ways, are just, A GOD of faithfulness and without perversity, Right and fair, is he!
text.KJV: 32:4 He is the rocke, his worke is perfect: for all his wayes are Iudgement: A God of trueth, and without iniquity, iust and right is he.
text.DRV: 32:4 The works of God be perfect, and all his ways judgments: God is faithful, and without any iniquity, just and right.

text.NRSV:
yet his degenerate children have dealt falsely with him,
	a perverse and crooked generation.
text.Rotherham: They have broken faith with him to be no sons of his—their fault,—A generation twisted and crooked.
text.KJV: 32:5 They haue corrupted themselues, their spot is not the spot of his children: they are a peruerse and crooked generation.
text.DRV: 32:5 They have sinned to him, and not his children in filthiness: a froward and perverse generation,

text.NRSV:
Do you thus repay the Lord,
	O foolish and senseless people?
Is not he your father, who created you,
	who made you and established you?
text.Rotherham: Is it Yahweh, ye thus requite, O impious people and unwise? Is not, he, thy father who begat thee? He, that made thee and established thee?
text.KJV: 32:6 Doe ye thus requite the Lord, O foolish people, & vnwise? Is not he thy Father that hath bought thee? Hath he not made thee, and established thee?
text.DRV: 32:6 These things doest thou render to our Lord, thou foolish and unwise people? Is not he thy father, that hath possessed thee, and made, and created thee?

text.Rotherham: Remember the days of age-past times, Remark the years of many generations,—Ask thy father and he will tell thee, Thine elders and they will say to thee:—
text.KJV: 32:7 ¶ Remember the dayes of olde, consider the yeeres of many generations: aske thy father, and he will shewe thee, thy Elders, and they wil tell thee.
text.DRV: 32:7 Remember the old days, think upon every generation: ask thy father, and he will declare to thee: thy elders, and they will tell thee.

text.NRSV:
When the Most High apportioned the nations,
	when he divided humankind,
he fixed the boundaries of the peoples
	according to the number of the gods;
text.Rotherham: When the Most High gave inheritances unto the nations, When he spread abroad the sons of Adam, He set the bounds of the peoples, According to the number of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 32:8 When the most High diuided to the nations their inheritance, when he separated the sonnes of Adam, hee set the bounds of the people according to the number of the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 32:8 When the highest divided the nations: when he separated the sons of Adam, he appointed the limits of people according to the number of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV:
the Lord’s own portion was his people,
	Jacob his allotted share.
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh’s portion is his people,—Jacob, his inherited possession.
text.KJV: 32:9 For the Lords portion is his people: Iacob is the lot of his inheritance.
text.DRV: 32:9 But our Lord's part is his people: Jacob the cord of his inheritance.

text.NRSV:
He sustained him in a desert land,
	in a howling wilderness waste;
he shielded him, cared for him,
	guarded him as the apple of his eye.
text.Rotherham: He met him in a desert land, And in the howling waste of a wilderness,—He encompassed him watched over him, Shielded him as the pupil of his own eye.
text.KJV: 32:10 He found him in a desert land, and in the waste howling wildernesse: Hee ledde him about, he instructed him, hee kept him as the apple of his eye.
text.DRV: 32:10 He found him in a desert land, and a place of horror, and of vast wilderness: he led him about, and taught him: and kept him as the apple of his eye.

text.NRSV:
As an eagle stirs up its nest,
	and hovers over its young;
as it spreads its wings, takes them up,
	and bears them aloft on its pinions,
text.Rotherham: As, an eagle, stirreth up his nest, Over his young ones, fluttereth, Spreadeth abroad his wings, taketh one, Beareth it up on his pinions,
text.KJV: 32:11 As an Eagle stirreth vp her nest, fluttereth ouer her yong, spreadeth abroad her wings, taketh them, beareth them on her wings:
text.DRV: 32:11 As the eagle provoking her young to fly, and hovering over them hath he spread his wings, and he hath taken him, and carried him on his shoulders.

text.NRSV:
the Lord alone guided him;
	no foreign god was with him.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh alone did lead him,—And there was with him no GOD of a stranger.
text.KJV: 32:12 So the Lord alone did leade him, and there was no strange God with him.
text.DRV: 32:12 Our Lord only was his guide: and there was not with him a strange god.

text.Rotherham: He made him ride on the high places of the land, Caused him to eat the increase of the fields,—And gave him to suck honey out of the cliff, And oil out of the rock of flint:
text.KJV: 32:13 He made him ride on the high places of the earth, that he might eate the increase of the fields, and he made him to sucke hony out of the rocke, and oyle out of the flintie rocke,
text.DRV: 32:13 He placed him over an high land: that he might eat the fruits of the fields, that he might suck honey out of the rock, and oil out of the hardest stone.

text.NRSV:
curds from the herd, and milk from the flock,
	with fat of lambs and rams;
Bashan bulls and goats, together with the choicest wheat—
	you drank fine wine from the blood of grapes.
text.Rotherham: Curds of kine and milk of sheep,—With fat of well-fed lambs, Yea rams bred in Bashan, and he-goats, With the white of the kernels of wheat,—And, the blood of the grape, thou didst drink as it foamed.
text.KJV: 32:14 Butter of kine, & milke of sheepe, with fat of lambes, and rammes of the breed of Bashan, & goats, with the fat of kidneis of wheat, and thou diddest drinke the pure blood of the grape.
text.DRV: 32:14 Butter from the herd, and milk of the sheep, with the fat of lambs, & of rams of the sons of Basan: and buck-goats with the marrow of wheat, and might drink the blood of the grape most pure.

text.NRSV: Jacob ate his fill; Jeshurun grew fat, and kicked. You grew fat, bloated, and gorged! He abandoned God who made him, and scoffed at the Rock of his salvation.
text.Rotherham: Then Jeshurun waxed fat, and kicked: Thou wast fat, Thou wast thick, Thou wast gorged, So he forsook the GOD who made him, And treated as foolish his Rock of salvation.
text.KJV: 32:15 ¶ But Iesurun waxed fat, and kicked: Thou art waxen fat, thou art growen thicke, thou art couered with fatnes: then he forsooke God which made him, and lightly esteemed the Rocke of his saluation.
text.DRV: 32:15 The beloved was made gross, and spurned: made gross, fatted, dilated, he left God his Maker, and departed from God his salvation.

text.NRSV: They made him jealous with strange gods, with abhorrent things they provoked him.
text.Rotherham: They moved him to jealousy with foreign [gods],—With abominations, angered they him:
text.KJV: 32:16 They prouoked him to ielousie with strange gods, with abominations prouoked they him to anger.
text.DRV: 32:16 They provoked him in strange gods, and in abominations stirred him to anger.

text.NRSV: They sacrificed to demons, not God, to deities they had never known, to new ones recently arrived, whom your ancestors had not feared.
text.Rotherham: They sacrificed to mischievous demons to a No-GOD, Gods whom they knew not,—New ones lately come in, Your fathers never shuddered at them!
text.KJV: 32:17 They sacrificed vnto deuils, not to God: to gods whom they knew not, to new gods, that came newly vp, whom your fathers feared not.
text.DRV: 32:17 They immolated to devils and not to God, to gods, which they knew not: there came new and fresh ones, whom their fathers worshipped not.

text.NRSV: You were unmindful of the Rock that bore you; you forgot the God who gave you birth.
text.Rotherham: Of the Rock who had begotten thee, thou wast unmindful,—And didst forget GOD who had given thee birth.
text.KJV: 32:18 Of the Rocke that begate thee thou art vnmindfull, and hast forgotten God that formed thee.
text.DRV: 32:18 God that begat thee thou hast forsaken, and hast forgotten our Lord thy creator.

text.NRSV: The Lord saw it, and was jealous; he spurned his sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: When Yahweh saw, he derided,—Because his sons and his daughters gave provocation.
text.KJV: 32:19 And when the Lord saw it, he abhorred them, because of the prouoking of his sonnes, & of his daughters.
text.DRV: 32:19 Our Lord saw, and was moved to wrath: because his sons and daughters provoked him.

text.NRSV: He said: I will hide my face from them, I will see what their end will be; for they are a perverse generation, children in whom there is no faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: So he said:—I will hide my face from them, I will see what will be their hereafter,—For a generation given to intrigue, they are, Sons, whom there is no trusting;
text.KJV: 32:20 And he said, I will hide my face from them, I will see what their ende shall be: for they are a very froward generation, children in whom is no faith.
text.DRV: 32:20 And he said: I will hide my face from them, and will consider their last: for it is a perverse generation, and unfaithful children.

text.NRSV: They made me jealous with what is no god, provoked me with their idols. So I will make them jealous with what is no people, provoke them with a foolish nation.
text.Rotherham: They have moved me to jealousy with a No-GOD, They have angered me with their vanities: I, therefore, will move them to jealousy with a No-people, With an impious nation, will I anger them.
text.KJV: 32:21 They haue mooued me to ielousie with that which is not god, they haue prouoked me to anger with their vanities: And I will moue them to ielousie with those which are not a people, I will prouoke them to anger with a foolish nation.
text.DRV: 32:21 They have provoked me in that, which was no God, and have angered me in their vanities: & I will provoke them in that which is no people, and in a foolish nation will I anger them.

text.NRSV: For a fire is kindled by my anger, and burns to the depths of Sheol; it devours the earth and its increase, and sets on fire the foundations of the mountains.
text.Rotherham: For, a fire, is kindled in mine anger, And shall burn as far as hades beneath,—And consume the earth with her produce, And set ablaze the foundations of the mountains:
text.KJV: 32:22 For a fire is kindled in my anger, and shall burne vnto the lowest hell, and shall consume the earth with her increase, and set on fire the foundations of the mountaines.
text.DRV: 32:22 Afire is kindled in my wrath, and shall burn even to the lowest parts of hell: and shall devour the earth with her spring, and shall burn the foundations of mountains.

text.NRSV: I will heap disasters upon them, spend my arrows against them:
text.Rotherham: I will heap on them, calamities,—Mine arrows, will I spend upon them:
text.KJV: 32:23 I will heape mischiefes vpon them, I will spend mine arrowes vpon them.
text.DRV: 32:23 I will heap evils upon them, and mine arrows I will spend in them.

text.NRSV: wasting hunger, burning consumption, bitter pestilence. The teeth of beasts I will send against them, with venom of things crawling in the dust.
text.Rotherham: The meltings of hunger, The devourings of fever, And the dangerous pestilence,—And, the tooth of beasts, will I send among them, With the poison of crawlers of the dust:
text.KJV: 32:24 They shall bee burnt with hunger and deuoured with burning heat, and with bitter destruction: I will also send the teeth of beasts vpon them, with the poison of serpents of the dust.
text.DRV: 32:24 They shall be consumed with famine, & birds shall devour them with most bitter biting: the teeth of beasts will I send upon them, with the fury of those that trail upon the ground, and creep.

text.NRSV: In the street the sword shall bereave, and in the chambers terror, for young man and woman alike, nursing child and old gray head.
text.Rotherham: Without, shall the sword bereave, In the inmost recesses, terror,—To both young man and virgin, Suckling, with man of grey hairs.
text.KJV: 32:25 The sword without, and terrour within shall destroy both the yong man, and the virgin, the suckling also with the man of gray haires.
text.DRV: 32:25 Without shall the sword devour them, and within fearfulness, the young man and the virgin together, the sucking child with the old man.

text.NRSV: I thought to scatter them and blot out the memory of them from humankind;
text.Rotherham: I said, I would puff them away, I would destroy from mortals, their memory;
text.KJV: 32:26 I said, I would scatter them into corners, I would make the remembrance of them to cease frō among men:
text.DRV: 32:26 Isaid: Where are they? I will make their memory to cease from among man.

text.NRSV: but I feared provocation by the enemy, for their adversaries might misunderstand and say, "Our hand is triumphant; it was not the Lord who did all this."
text.Rotherham: Were it not that the taunt of the foe, I feared, Lest their adversaries should mistake,—Lest they should say—Our own hand, is exalted, It is not Yahweh, therefore who hath wrought all this!
text.KJV: 32:27 Were it not that I feared the wrath of the enemie, lest their aduersaries should behaue themselues strangely, and lest they should say, Our hande is high, and the Lord hath not done all this.
text.DRV: 32:27 But for the wrath of the enemies I have deferred: lest perhaps their enemies might be proud, & would say: Our mighty hand, and not the Lord, hath done all these things.

text.NRSV: They are a nation void of sense; there is no understanding in them.
text.Rotherham: For a nation of vanished sagacity, they are,—And there is in them no understanding.
text.KJV: 32:28 For they are a nation voide of counsel, neither is there any vnderstanding in them.
text.DRV: 32:28 Anation without counsel is it, and without wisdom.

text.NRSV: If they were wise, they would understand this; they would discern what the end would be.
text.Rotherham: If they had been wise, they would have understood this,—They would have given heed to their here-after!
text.KJV: 32:29 O that they were wise, that they vnderstood this, that they would consider their latter end.
text.DRV: 32:29 O that they were wise, and understood, and would provide for their last!

text.NRSV: How could one have routed a thousand, and two put a myriad to flight, unless their Rock had sold them, the Lord had given them up?
text.Rotherham: Oh! how would one, have chased, a thousand! And, two, put, ten thousand to flight,—Were it not that their Rock had sold them, And, Yahweh, had abandoned them;
text.KJV: 32:30 How should one chase a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight, except their Rocke had sold them, and the Lord had shut them vp?
text.DRV: 32:30 How should one pursue a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight? Was it not therefore, because their God sold them, and our Lord inclosed them?

text.NRSV: Indeed their rock is not like our Rock; our enemies are fools.
text.Rotherham: For, not like our Rock, is their rock, Our enemies themselves, being judges;
text.KJV: 32:31 For their rocke is not as our Rocke, euen our enemies themselues being iudges.
text.DRV: 32:31 For our Lord is not as their gods: our enemies also are judges.

text.NRSV: Their vine comes from the vinestock of Sodom, from the vineyards of Gomorrah; their grapes are grapes of poison, their clusters are bitter;
text.Rotherham: For of the vine of Sodom, is their vine, And of the vineyards of Gomorrah,—Their grapes, are grapes of gall, Clusters of bitter things, are theirs:
text.KJV: 32:32 For their vine is of the vine of Sodome, and of the fields of Gomorah: their grapes are grapes of gall, their clusters are bitter.
text.DRV: 32:32 Of the vineyard of Sodom, is their vineyard, and of the suburbs of Gomorrha: their grape the grape of gall, and the clusters most bitter.

text.NRSV: their wine is the poison of serpents, the cruel venom of asps.
text.Rotherham: The poison of large serpents, is their wine,—Yea the fierce venom of asps.
text.KJV: 32:33 Their wine is the poison of dragons, and the cruell venime of Aspes.
text.DRV: 32:33 The gall of dragons their wine, and the venom of Asps, uncurable.

text.NRSV: Is not this laid up in store with me, sealed up in my treasuries?
text.Rotherham: Is not, that, stored up with me,—Sealed up amongst my treasures;
text.KJV: 32:34 Is not this laide vp in store with me, and sealed vp among my treasures?
text.DRV: 32:34 Are not these things laid up with me, and signed in my treasures?

text.NRSV: Vengeance is mine, and recompense, for the time when their foot shall slip; because the day of their calamity is at hand, their doom comes swiftly.
text.Rotherham: Unto the days of vengeance and requital: Unto the time their foot shall totter? For, near, is the day of their fate, And their destiny speedeth on.
text.KJV: 32:35 To me belongeth vengeance, and recompence, their foot shall slide in due time: for the day of their calamitie is at hand, and the things that shal come vpon them, make haste.
text.DRV: 32:35 Revenge is mine, and I will repay them in time, that their foot may slide: the day of perdition is at hand, and the times make haste to be present.

text.NRSV: Indeed the Lord will vindicate his people, have compassion on his servants, when he sees that their power is gone, neither bond nor free remaining.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh will vindicate his people, And upon his servants, will have compassion,—When he seeth that strength is exhausted, And there is no one shut up or at large,
text.KJV: 32:36 For the Lord shall iudge his people, and repent himselfe for his seruants, when he seeth that their power is gone; and there is none shut vp, or left.
text.DRV: 32:36 Our Lord will judge his people, and will have mercy on his servants: he shall see that their hand is weakened, & the shut-up also have failed, and the residue be consumed.

text.NRSV: Then he will say: Where are their gods, the rock in which they took refuge,
text.Rotherham: Then will he say, Where are their gods, The rock in whom they have trusted;
text.KJV: 32:37 And he shall say, where are their gods? their Rocke in whom they trusted;
text.DRV: 32:37 And he shall say: Where are their gods, in whom they had confidence.

text.NRSV: who ate the fat of their sacrifices, and drank the wine of their libations? Let them rise up and help you, let them be your protection!
text.Rotherham: Who used to eat the fat of their sacrifices, To drink the wine of their libations? Let them rise up and help you, Let them be over you for a coveting!
text.KJV: 32:38 Which did eat the fat of their sacrifices, & dranke the wine of their drinke offerings? let them rise vp and helpe you, and be your protection.
text.DRV: 32:38 Of whose victims they did eat fat, and drank the wine of their libaments: Let them arise, and help you, and protect you in necessity.

text.NRSV: See now that I, even I, am he; there is no god besides me. I kill and I make alive; I wound and I heal; and no one can deliver from my hand.
text.Rotherham: See, now, that, I, I, am, he that is, And there are no gods with me,—I, kill—and make alive, I wound and, I, heal, And there is none who, from my hand, can deliver,
text.KJV: 32:39 See now, that I, euen I am he, and there is no god with mee; I kill, and I make aliue: I wound, and I heale: neither is there any that can deliuer out of my hand.
text.DRV: 32:39 See ye that I am only, and there is no other God besides me: I will kill, and I will make to live: I will strike, and I will heal, and there is none that can deliver out of my hand.

text.NRSV: For I lift up my hand to heaven, and swear: As I live forever,
text.Rotherham: For I lift up unto the heavens my hand,—And say, Living am, I, unto times age-abiding:
text.KJV: 32:40 For I lift vp my hand to heauen, and say, I liue for euer.
text.DRV: 32:40 I will lift up my hand to heaven, and will say: I live for ever.

text.NRSV: when I whet my flashing sword, and my hand takes hold on judgment; I will take vengeance on my adversaries, and will repay those who hate me.
text.Rotherham: If I whet my flashing sword, And my hand take hold on justice, I will return vengeance unto mine adversaries, And them who hate me, will I requite:
text.KJV: 32:41 If I whet my glittering sword, and mine hand take holde on Iudgement, I will render vengeance to mine enemies, and will reward them that hate me.
text.DRV: 32:41 If I shall whet my sword as the lightning, & my hand take judgment: I will repay vengeance to mine enemies, and them that hate me will I requite.

text.NRSV: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh— with the blood of the slain and the captives, from the long-haired enemy.
text.Rotherham: I will make mine arrows drunk with blood, And, my sword, shall devour flesh,—With the blood of the slain and the captive, With the flesh of the chief leader of the foe.
text.KJV: 32:42 I will make mine arrowes drunke with blood, (and my sword shal deuoure flesh) and that with the blood of the slaine, and of the captiues, from the beginning of reuenges vpon the enemie.
text.DRV: 32:42 I will imbrue my arrows with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh, of the blood of the slain and captivity, of the bare head of the enemies.

text.NRSV: Praise, O heavens, his people, worship him, all you gods! For he will avenge the blood of his children, and take vengeance on his adversaries; he will repay those who hate him, and cleanse the land for his people.
text.Rotherham: Shout for joy O ye nations [with] his people, For the blood of his servants, he avengeth,—And, vengeance, he returneth unto his adversaries, And is propitious unto the soil of his people.
text.KJV: 32:43 Reioyce, O ye nations with his people, for he will auenge the blood of his seruants, and will render vengeance to his aduersaries, and wil be mercifull vnto his land, and to his people.
text.DRV: 32:43 You gentiles praise his people, because he will revenge the blood of his servants: and will repay vengeance upon their enemies, and will be propitious to the land of his people.

text.NRSV: Moses came and recited all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he and Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: So Moses came in and spake all the words of this Song in the hearing of the people,—he and Hoshea son of Nun.
text.KJV: 32:44 ¶ And Moses came and spake all the wordes of this song in the eares of the people, he and Hoshea the sonne of Nun.
text.DRV: 32:44 Moyses therefore came and spake all the words of this canticle in the ears of the people, he and Josue the son of Nun.

text.NRSV: When Moses had finished reciting all these words to all Israel,
text.Rotherham: And, when Moses made an end of speaking all these words unto all Israel,
text.KJV: 32:45 And Moses made an end of speaking all these words to all Israel.
text.DRV: 32:45 And he finished all these words, speaking to the children of Israel,

text.NRSV: he said to them: "Take to heart all the words that I am giving in witness against you today; give them as a command to your children, so that they may diligently observe all the words of this law.
text.Rotherham: he said unto them—Apply your hearts unto all the words which I am witnessing against you, to-day,—how that ye must command your sons to observe to do all the words of this law;
text.KJV: 32:46 And hee said vnto them, Set your hearts vnto all the wordes which I testifie among you this day, which yee shall commaund your children to obserue to doe all the wordes of this Law.
text.DRV: 32:46 & he said to them: Set your hearts on all the words, which I testify to you this day: that you command them to your children to keep and to do, and to fulfill all things of this law that are written:

text.NRSV: This is no trifling matter for you, but rather your very life; through it you may live long in the land that you are crossing over the Jordan to possess."
text.Rotherham: for it is not, too small a thing, for you, for, it, is your life,—and, hereby, shall ye prolong your days upon the soil whereunto ye are, passing over the Jordan to possess it.
text.KJV: 32:47 For it is not a vaine thing for you: because it is your life, and through this thing yee shall prolong your dayes, in the land whither yee goe ouer Iordan to possesse it.
text.DRV: 32:47 for not in vain are they commanded you, but that every one should live in them: which doing you may continue a long time in the Land, which passing over Jordan you enter to possess.

text.NRSV: On that very day the Lord addressed Moses as follows:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, on this selfsame day, saying:
text.KJV: 32:48 And the Lord spake vnto Moses that selfe same day, saying,
text.DRV: 32:48 And our Lord spake to Moyses in the same day, saying:

text.NRSV: "Ascend this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, across from Jericho, and view the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites for a possession;
text.Rotherham: Get thee up into this mountain of the Abarim, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, which is over against Jericho—and behold the land of Canaan which I am giving unto the sons of Israel, for a possession;
text.KJV: 32:49 Get thee vp into this mountaine Abarim, vnto mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, that is ouer against Iericho, and behold the land of Canaan which I giue vnto the children of Israel for a possession:
text.DRV: 32:49 Go up into this mount Abarim, that is to say, of passages, into mount Nebo, which is in the Land of Moab against Jericho: and see the Land of Chanaan, which I will deliver to the children of Israel to obtain, and die thou in the mount.

text.NRSV: you shall die there on the mountain that you ascend and shall be gathered to your kin, as your brother Aaron died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his kin;
text.Rotherham: and die thou in the mount whither thou art going up, and be withdrawn unto thy kinsfolk,—as Aaron thy brother died in Mount Hor, and was withdrawn unto his kinsfolk;
text.KJV: 32:50 And die in the mount whither thou goest vp, and bee gathered vnto thy people, as Aaron thy brother died in mount Hor, and was gathered vnto his people:
text.DRV: 32:50 Which going up unto thou shalt be joined to thy peoples, as Aaron thy brother died in mount Hor, and was laid to his people:

text.NRSV: because both of you broke faith with me among the Israelites at the waters of Meribath-kadesh in the wilderness of Zin, by failing to maintain my holiness among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: for that ye acted unfaithfully against me in the midst of the sons of Israel, regarding the waters of Meribah-Kadesh in the desert of Zin,—for that ye hallowed me not in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 32:51 Because ye trespassed against me among the children of Israel, at the waters of Meribah Kadesh, in the wildernesse of Zin: because yee sanctified mee not in the midst of the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 32:51 because you did prevaricate against me in the midst of the children of Israel at the Waters of contradiction in Cades of the desert of Sin: and you did not sanctify me among the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Although you may view the land from a distance, you shall not enter it—the land that I am giving to the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: Yet, over against thee, shalt thou behold the land,—but thereinto, shalt thou not enter, into the land which I am giving unto the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 32:52 Yet thou shalt see the land before thee, but thou shalt not goe thither vnto the land which I giue the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 32:52 Over against shalt thou see the Land, and shalt not enter into it, which I will give to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: This is the blessing with which Moses, the man of God, blessed the Israelites before his death.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, is the blessing wherewith Moses, the man of God, blessed the sons of Israel,—before his death.
text.KJV: 33:1 And this is the blessing, wherewith Moses the man of God blessed the children of Israel before his death.
text.DRV: 33:1 This is the blessing, wherewith Moyses the man of God blessed the children of Israel before his death.

text.Rotherham: And he said—Yahweh from Sinai, came near,—Yea he dawned, out of Seir, upon them, He shone forth out of Mount Paran, Yea he came out of holy myriads,—Out of his right hand [proceeded] fire to guide them.
text.KJV: 33:2 And he said, The Lord came from Sinai, and rose vp from Seir vnto them, hee shined foorth from mount Paran, and hee came with ten thousands of Saints: from his Right hand went a fierie Law for them.
text.DRV: 33:2 And he said: Our Lord came from Sinai, and from Seir is he risen to us: he hath appeared from mount Pharan, and with him thousands of Saints. In his right hand a fiery law.

text.NRSV:
Indeed, O favorite among peoples,
	all his holy ones were in your charge;
they marched at your heels,
	accepted direction from you.
text.Rotherham: Yea he loved the tribes, All his holy ones, were in thy hand,—Yea, they, were encamped at thy feet, Each one bare away some of thy words.
text.KJV: 33:3 Yea hee loued the people; all his Saints are in thy hand: and they sate downe at thy feete; euery one shall receiue of thy wordes.
text.DRV: 33:3 He hath loved the peoples, all the Saints are in his hand: and they that approach to his feet shall receive of his doctrine.

text.NRSV:
Moses charged us with the law,
	as a possession for the assembly of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: A law, did Moses command us,—A possession, for the convocation of Jacob.
text.KJV: 33:4 Moses commaunded vs a Law, euen the inheritance of the Congregation of Iacob.
text.DRV: 33:4 Moyses commanded us a law, the inheritance of the multitude of Jacob.

text.NRSV:
There arose a king in Jeshurun,
	when the leaders of the people assembled—
	the united tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Thus became he in, Jeshurun, a king,—When the heads of the people gathered them-selves together, As one man, the tribes of Israel. [Reuben] —
text.KJV: 33:5 And hee was King in Iesurun, when the heads of the people, and the Tribes of Israel were gathered together.
text.DRV: 33:5 He shall be king with the most right, the Princes of the people being assembled with the tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV:
May Reuben live, and not die out,
	even though his numbers are few.
text.Rotherham: Let Reuben live and not die,—Nor his men be easily counted.
text.KJV: 33:6 ¶ Let Reuben liue, and not die, and let not his men be few.
text.DRV: 33:6 Live Ruben, and die he not, and be he little in number.

text.NRSV:
And this he said of Judah:
O Lord, give heed to Judah,
	and bring him to his people;
strengthen his hands for him,
	and be a help against his adversaries.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is for Judah, when he said, Hear, O Yahweh, the voice of Judah, That unto his own people, thou wouldst bring him home,—Let, his own hands, suffice him, And, a helper from his adversaries, do thou become.
text.KJV: 33:7 ¶ And this is the blessing of Iudah: and he said, Heare, Lord, the voice of Iudah, and bring him vnto his people: let his hands bee sufficient for him, and bee thou an helpe to him from his enemies.
text.DRV: 33:7 This is the blessing of Judas: Hear Lord the voice of Judas, and bring him in unto his people: his hands shall fight for him, and he shall be his helper against his adversaries.

text.NRSV: And of Levi he said: Give to Levi your Thummim, and your Urim to your loyal one, whom you tested at Massah, with whom you contended at the waters of Meribah;
text.Rotherham: And of Levi, he said: Let, thy perfections and thy lights, belong unto thy man of lovingkindness,—Whom thou didst put to the proof at Massah, With whom thou didst contend over the waters of Meribah;
text.KJV: 33:8 ¶ And of Leui hee said, Let thy Thummim and thy Urim be with thy holy one, whom thou diddest prooue at Massah, & with whom thou didst striue at the waters of Meribah:
text.DRV: 33:8 To Levi also he said: Thy perfection, and thy doctrine be to thy holy man, whom thou hast proved in tentation, and judged at the Waters of contradiction.

text.NRSV: who said of his father and mother, "I regard them not"; he ignored his kin, and did not acknowledge his children. For they observed your word, and kept your covenant.
text.Rotherham: Who saith of his father and of his mother, I behold them not, And, his own brethren, regardeth he not, And, his own sons, observeth he not,—Because they watch thy speech, That, thy covenant, they may preserve,;
text.KJV: 33:9 Who said vnto his father & to his mother, I haue not seene him, neither did hee acknowledge his brethren; nor knew his owne children: for they haue obserued thy word, and kept thy Couenant.
text.DRV: 33:9 He that said to his father and to his mother: I know you not; and to his brethren: I know you not: and they knew not their children. These kept thy word, and observed thy covenant,

text.NRSV: They teach Jacob your ordinances, and Israel your law; they place incense before you, and whole burnt offerings on your altar.
text.Rotherham: May teach thy regulations unto Jacob, And thy law unto Israel,—May put incense in thy nostrils, And a whole-offering upon thine altar.
text.KJV: 33:10 They shal teach Iacob thy iudgments, and Israel thy Lawe: they shall put incense before thee, and whole burnt sacrifice vpon thine Altar.
text.DRV: 33:10 thy judgments, o Jacob, and thy law, o Israel: they shall put incense in thy fury, and holocaust upon thine altar.

text.NRSV: Bless, O Lord, his substance, and accept the work of his hands; crush the loins of his adversaries, of those that hate him, so that they do not rise again.
text.Rotherham: Bless, O Yahweh his substance, And with the work of his hands, wilt thou be well pleased,—Crush thou the loins of them who rise up against him, And of them who hate him, that they rise not again.
text.KJV: 33:11 Blesse, Lord, his substance, and accept the worke of his handes, smite thorow the loines of them that rise against him, and of them that hate him, that they rise not againe.
text.DRV: 33:11 Bless Lord his strength, and receive the works of his hands. Strike the backs of his enemies, and they that hate him, let them not rise up.

text.NRSV: Of Benjamin he said: The beloved of the Lord rests in safety— the High God surrounds him all day long— the beloved rests between his shoulders.
text.Rotherham: Of Benjamin, he said, The beloved of Yahweh, shall settle down securely,—With one to throw a shade over him all the day long, Yea, between his shoulders, hath he found rest.
text.KJV: 33:12 ¶ And of Beniamin he said, The beloued of the Lord shall dwell in safetie by him, and the Lord shall couer him all the day long, and he shall dwell betweene his shoulders.
text.DRV: 33:12 And to Benjamin he said: The best beloved of our Lord shall dwell confidently in him: as in a bride-chamber all the day shall he abide, and between his shoulders shall he rest.

text.NRSV: And of Joseph he said: Blessed by the Lord be his land, with the choice gifts of heaven above, and of the deep that lies beneath;
text.Rotherham: And of Joseph, he said, Blessed of Yahweh, be his land,—With the precious things of the heavens with the dew, And with the abyss, couching beneath;
text.KJV: 33:13 ¶ And of Ioseph he said, Blessed of the Lord be his land, for the precious things of heauen, for the dew, and for the deep that coucheth beneath;
text.DRV: 33:13 To Joseph also he said: Of the blessing of our Lord be his land, of the fruits of heaven, and the dew, & the depth lying underneath.

text.NRSV: with the choice fruits of the sun, and the rich yield of the months;
text.Rotherham: And with the precious fruits of the sun,—And with the precious yield of the moons;
text.KJV: 33:14 And for the precious fruits brought forth by the sunne, and for the precious things put forth by the moone,
text.DRV: 33:14 Of the pomes of the fruits of the sun and moon,

text.NRSV: with the finest produce of the ancient mountains, and the abundance of the everlasting hills;
text.Rotherham: And with the chief of the ancient mountains,—And with the precious things of the age-abiding hills;
text.KJV: 33:15 And for the chiefe things of the ancient mountaines, and for the precious things of the lasting hils,
text.DRV: 33:15 of the tops of the old mountains, of the pomes of the eternal hills:

text.NRSV: with the choice gifts of the earth and its fullness, and the favor of the one who dwells on Sinai. Let these come on the head of Joseph, on the brow of the prince among his brothers.
text.Rotherham: And with the precious things of the earth and its fulness, And with the goodwill of One who dwelt in a thorn-bush.—Let it come on the head of Joseph, And on the crown of the head of him who was consecrated among his brethren.
text.KJV: 33:16 And for the precious things of the earth, and fulnesse thereof, and for the good will of him that dwelt in the bush: let the blessing come vpon the head of Ioseph, and vpon the top of the head of him that was separated frō his brethren.
text.DRV: 33:16 and of the fruits of the earth, and of the fullness thereof. The blessing of him, that appeared in the bush, come upon the head of Joseph, and upon the crown of the Nazarite among his brethren.

text.NRSV: A firstborn bull—majesty is his! His horns are the horns of a wild ox; with them he gores the peoples, driving them to the ends of the earth; such are the myriads of Ephraim, such the thousands of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: His firstborn ox, be an honour to him. And the horns of a buffalo, be his horns, With them, let him thrust, peoples, All at once, to the ends of the earth,—Yea, [with] these, the myriads of Ephraim, And, [with] those, the thousands of Manasseh.
text.KJV: 33:17 His glory is like the firstling of his bullocke, & his hornes are like the hornes of Unicornes: with them he shall push the people together, to the ends of the earth: and they are the ten thousands of Ephraim, and they are the thousands of Manasseh.
text.DRV: 33:17 His beauty as of the first born of an oxe, his horns the horns of an unicorn: in them shall he winnow the Nations even to the ends of the earth. These are the multitudes of Ephraim, and these the thousands of Manasses.

text.NRSV: And of Zebulun he said: Rejoice, Zebulun, in your going out; and Issachar, in your tents.
text.Rotherham: And, of Zebulun, he said, Rejoice, Zebulun in thy going out,—And [thou], Issachar in thy tents:
text.KJV: 33:18 ¶ And of Zebulun he said, Reioyce, Zebulun, in thy going out; and Issachar, in thy tents.
text.DRV: 33:18 And to Zabulon he said: Rejoice Zabulon in thy going out, and Issachar in thy tabernacles.

text.NRSV: They call peoples to the mountain; there they offer the right sacrifices; for they suck the affluence of the seas and the hidden treasures of the sand.
text.Rotherham: The peoples, unto the mountain, will they call, There, will they offer the sacrifices of righteousness,—For, the abundance of the seas, will they suck, Hidden treasures of the sand.
text.KJV: 33:19 They shall call the people vnto the mountaine, there they shal offer sacrifices of righteousnesse: for they shall sucke of the abundance of the seas, and of treasures hid in the sand.
text.DRV: 33:19 They shall call the peoples to the mountain: there shall they immolate the victims of justice. Who shall suck the inundation of the sea as milk, and the hidden treasures of the sands.

text.NRSV: And of Gad he said: Blessed be the enlargement of Gad! Gad lives like a lion; he tears at arm and scalp.
text.Rotherham: And, of Gad, he said: Blessed, be he that hath made room for Gad,—As a lioness, hath he laid himself down, And hath torn an arm likewise the crown of the head;
text.KJV: 33:20 ¶ And of Gad he said, Blessed be he that enlargeth Gad: he dwelleth as a lyon, and teareth the arme with the crowne of the head.
text.DRV: 33:20 And to Gad he said: Blessed be Gad in breadth: as a lion hath he rested, and taken the arm and the top of the head.

text.NRSV: He chose the best for himself, for there a commander's allotment was reserved; he came at the head of the people, he executed the justice of the Lord, and his ordinances for Israel.
text.Rotherham: And hath provided a first part for himself, For, there, a commander’s portion hath been hid,—So he cometh with the heads of the people, The righteousness of Yahweh, he executeth, And his regulations, with Israel.
text.KJV: 33:21 And he prouided the first part for himselfe, because there, in a portion of the lawgiuer was he seated, and hee came with the heads of the people, he executed the iustice of the Lord, and his iudgments with Israel.
text.DRV: 33:21 And he saw his principality, that in his part the doctor was reposed: which was with the princes of the people, and did the justices of our Lord, and his judgment with Israel.

text.NRSV: And of Dan he said: Dan is a lion's whelp that leaps forth from Bashan.
text.Rotherham: And, of Dan, he said, Dan, is a lion’s whelp,—He leapeth out of Bashan.
text.KJV: 33:22 ¶ And of Dan he said, Dan is a Lyons whelpe: hee shall leape from Bashan.
text.DRV: 33:22 To Dan also he said: Dan a lion's whelp, he shall flow largely from Basan.

text.NRSV: And of Naphtali he said: O Naphtali, sated with favor, full of the blessing of the Lord, possess the west and the south.
text.Rotherham: And, of Naphtali, he said, O Naphtali! satisfied with favour, And full with the blessing of Yahweh,—The west and the sunny south, do thou possess.
text.KJV: 33:23 ¶ And of Naphtali he said, O Naphtali, satisfied with fauour, and full with the blessing of the Lord: possesse thou the West and the South.
text.DRV: 33:23 And to Nephthali he said: Nephthali shall enjoy abundance, and shall be full of the blessings of our Lord: the sea and the south he shall possess.

text.NRSV: And of Asher he said: Most blessed of sons be Asher; may he be the favorite of his brothers, and may he dip his foot in oil.
text.Rotherham: And, of Asher, he said, Most blessed of sons, be Asher,—Let him have the goodwill of his brethren, And let him dip, in oil, his foot:
text.KJV: 33:24 ¶ And of Asher hee said, let Asher be blessed with children, Let him be acceptable to his brethren, and let him dip his foot in oile.
text.DRV: 33:24 To Aser also he said: Blessed be Aser in children, be he acceptable to his brethren, and dip he in oil his foot.

text.NRSV: Your bars are iron and bronze; and as your days, so is your strength.
text.Rotherham: Iron and bronze, be thy sandals, And, as thy days, be thy strength.
text.KJV: 33:25 Thy shooes shall bee yron and brasse, and as thy dayes, so shall thy strength bee.
text.DRV: 33:25 His shoe iron and brass. As the days of thy youth, so also thy old age.

text.NRSV: There is none like God, O Jeshurun, who rides through the heavens to your help, majestic through the skies.
text.Rotherham: There is none like GOD, O Jeshurun,—Riding upon the heavens to thy help,—And for his own majesty, on the skies;
text.KJV: 33:26 ¶ There is none like vnto the God of Iesurun, who rideth vpon the heauen in thy helpe, and in his excellencie on the skie.
text.DRV: 33:26 There is no other God as the God of the rightest: the mounter of heaven is thy helper. By his magnificence the clouds run hither and thither,

text.NRSV: He subdues the ancient gods, shatters the forces of old; he drove out the enemy before you, and said, "Destroy!"
text.Rotherham: Above, is the God of aforetime, And, beneath, are the Arms of the ages,—So he driveth before thee the foe, And doth say—Destroy!
text.KJV: 33:27 The eternall God is thy refuge, and vnderneath are the euerlasting armes: and he shall thrust out the enemie from before thee, and shall say, Destroy them.
text.DRV: 33:27 his habitation is above, and under the everlasting arms: he shall cast out the enemy from thy face, and shall say: Be destroyed.

text.NRSV: So Israel lives in safety, untroubled is Jacob's abode in a land of grain and wine, where the heavens drop down dew.
text.Rotherham: Thus hath Israel settled down securely, Alone, the fountain of Jacob, Within a land both of corn and new wine,—His heaven also droppeth down dew.
text.KJV: 33:28 Israel then shall dwell in safetie alone: the fountaine of Iacob shalbe vpon a land of corne and wine, also his heauens shall drop downe deaw.
text.DRV: 33:28 Israel shall dwell confidently, and alone. The eye of Jacob in the land of corn and wine, and the heavens shall be misty with dew.

text.NRSV: Happy are you, O Israel! Who is like you, a people saved by the Lord, the shield of your help, and the sword of your triumph! Your enemies shall come fawning to you, and you shall tread on their backs.
text.Rotherham: How happy art thou, O Israel! Who is like unto thee, O people victorious in Yahweh? Thy helpful shield, who also is thy majestic sword,—So that thy foes come cringing unto thee, And, thou, upon their heights, dost march along.
text.KJV: 33:29 Happy art thou, O Israel: Who is like vnto thee, O people! saued by the Lord, the shield of thy helpe, and who is the sword of thy excellencie: and thine enemies shal be found liars vnto thee, and thou shalt tread vpon their high places.
text.DRV: 33:29 Blessed art thou Israel: who is like to thee, o people that art saved in our Lord, the shield of thy help, and the sword of thy glory? Thy enemies shall deny thee, and thou shalt tread their necks.

text.NRSV: Then Moses went up from the plains of Moab to Mount Nebo, to the top of Pisgah, which is opposite Jericho, and the Lord showed him the whole land: Gilead as far as Dan,
text.Rotherham: So then Moses went up, from the waste plains of Moab into Mount Nebo, the head of Pisgah, which is over against Jericho,—and Yahweh showed him all the land—even Gilead as far as Dan;
text.KJV: 34:1 And Moses went vp from the plaines of Moab, vnto the mountaine of Nebo, to the top of Pisgah, that is ouer against Iericho: and the Lord shewed him all the land of Gilead, vnto Dan,
text.DRV: 34:1 Moyses therefore went up from the champion of Moab upon mount Nebo, into the top of Phasga against Jericho: and our Lord shewed him all the land of Galaad as far as Dan,

text.NRSV: all Naphtali, the land of Ephraim and Manasseh, all the land of Judah as far as the Western Sea,
text.Rotherham: and all Naphtali, and the land of Ephraim and Manasseh,—and all the land of Judah, as far as the hinder sea;
text.KJV: 34:2 And all Naphtali, and the lande of Ephraim, and Manasseh, and all the land of Iudah, vnto the vtmost sea.
text.DRV: 34:2 and all Nephthali, and the land of Ephraim and Manasses, and all the Land of Juda unto the utmost sea,

text.NRSV: the Negeb, and the Plain—that is, the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees—as far as Zoar.
text.Rotherham: and the South,—and the circuit of the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees as far as Zoar.
text.KJV: 34:3 And the South, and the plaine of the valley of Iericho, the citie of palme trees vnto Zoar.
text.DRV: 34:3 and the south part, and the breadth of the plain of Jericho, a city of palm-trees as far as Segor.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to him, “This is the land of which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, ‘I will give it to your descendants’; I have let you see it with your eyes, but you shall not cross over there.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him—This, is the land which I sware to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed, will I give it. I have let thee see with thine own eyes, But thither, shalt thou not pass over.
text.KJV: 34:4 And the Lord said vnto him, This is the land which I sware vnto Abraham, vnto Isaac, and vnto Iacob, saying, I will giue it vnto thy seed: I haue caused thee to see it with thine eyes, but thou shalt not go ouer thither.
text.DRV: 34:4 And our Lord said to him: This is the Land, for the which I sware to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying: To thy seed will I give it. Thou hast seen it with thine eyes, and shalt not pass over to it.

text.NRSV: Then Moses, the servant of the Lord, died there in the land of Moab, at the Lord’s command.
text.Rotherham: So Moses, the servant of Yahweh, died there, in the land of Moab, at the bidding of Yahweh;
text.KJV: 34:5 ¶ So Moses the seruant of the Lord died there in the land of Moab, according to the word of the Lord.
text.DRV: 34:5 And Moyses the servant of our Lord died there, in the land of Moab, our Lord commanding it:

text.NRSV: He was buried in a valley in the land of Moab, opposite Beth-peor, but no one knows his burial place to this day.
text.Rotherham: and he buried him, in a valley in the land of Moab, over against Beth-peor,—but no man hath known his burying-place, until this day.
text.KJV: 34:6 And hee buried him in a valley in the land of Moab, ouer against Beth-Peor: but no man knoweth of his Sepulchre vnto this day.
text.DRV: 34:6 and he buried him in the valley of the Land of Moab against Phogor: and no man hath known his sepulchre until this present day.

text.NRSV: Moses was one hundred twenty years old when he died; his sight was unimpaired and his vigor had not abated.
text.Rotherham: Now, Moses, was a hundred and twenty years old, when he died,—his eye had not dimmed, nor had his freshness fled.
text.KJV: 34:7 ¶ And Moses was an hundred and twentie yeeres olde when he died: his eye was not dimme, nor his naturall force abated.
text.DRV: 34:7 Moyses was an hundred and twenty years old when he died: his eye was not dim, neither were his teeth moved.

text.NRSV: The Israelites wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days; then the period of mourning for Moses was ended.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel bewailed Moses in the waste plains of Moab thirty days,—then were completed the wailing-days of the mourning for Moses.
text.KJV: 34:8 ¶ And the children of Israel wept for Moses in the plaines of Moab thirty dayes: So the dayes of weeping and mourning for Moses were ended.
text.DRV: 34:8 And the children of Israel mourned him in the champion country of Moab thirty days: & the days of their mourning that mourned for Moyses were accomplished.

text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom, because Moses had laid his hands on him; and the Israelites obeyed him, doing as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua, son of Nun, was full of the spirit of wisdom, for Moses had laid his hands upon him,—so the sons of Israel hearkened unto him, and did, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 34:9 ¶ And Ioshua the sonne of Nun was full of the Spirit of wisedome: for Moses had layd his handes vpon him, and the children of Israel hearkened vnto him, and did as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.DRV: 34:9 And Josue the son of Nun was replenished with the spirit of wisdom, because Moyses did put his hands upon him. And the children of Israel obeyed him, and did as our Lord commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: Never since has there arisen a prophet in Israel like Moses, whom the Lord knew face to face.
text.Rotherham: And there arose not a prophet any more in Israel, like unto Moses,—whom Yahweh acknowledged, face to face;
text.KJV: 34:10 ¶ And there arose not a Prophet since in Israel like vnto Moses, whom the Lord knew face to face:
text.DRV: 34:10 And there rose no more a Prophet in Israel as Moyses, whom our Lord had known face to face,

text.NRSV: He was unequaled for all the signs and wonders that the Lord sent him to perform in the land of Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants and his entire land,
text.Rotherham: by all the signs and the wonders which Yahweh sent him to do in the land of Egypt,—unto Pharaoh and unto all his servants and unto all his land;
text.KJV: 34:11 In al the signes and the wonders which the Lord sent him to doe in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh, and to all his seruants, and to all his land,
text.DRV: 34:11 in all signs and wonders, which he sent by him, to do in the Land of Ægypt to Pharao, and to all his servants, and to his whole Land,

text.NRSV: and for all the mighty deeds and all the terrifying displays of power that Moses performed in the sight of all Israel.
text.Rotherham: and by all the firm hand, and by all the great terror which Moses wrought in the sight of all Israel.
text.KJV: 34:12 And in all that mighty hand, and in all the great terrour, which Moses shewed in the sight of all Israel.
text.DRV: 34:12 and all the strong hand, and great marvels, which Moyses did before all Israel.
